entries.title: Astana ends on a high with a heavyweight finale
entries.description: The Qazaqstan Barysy Grand Slam 2026 ended in style as the heavyweights closed the show in Astana. Boris Rutovic, Anna Monta Olek, Marat Baikamurov, Elis Startseva and Inal Tasoev took gold, while Kazakhstan finished strongly with four medals and third in the table.

entries.title: How much would you pay to see Ronaldo? Here’s the cheapest ticket for his 2026 World Cup game
entries.description: The match between Portugal and Colombia in the 2026 World Cup is becoming a phenomenon off the pitch. The anticipation surrounding what could be Cristiano Ronaldo's last World Cup has caused the price of the cheapest tickets to explode.

entries.title: Real Madrid's president slams 'organised campaign' to unseat him in fiery presser
entries.description: Madrid has endured a second consecutive season without a major trophy, despite having France star Kylian Mbappé in the squad.

entries.title: Barcelona star Lamine Yamal waves Palestinian flag during La Liga celebrations
entries.description: Footage released on May 12 showed Yamal waving a Palestinian flag during FC Barcelona’s La Liga title parade celebrating the club’s 29th league title. In a news conference, Barcelona coach Hansi Flick said he did not like such gestures, but added that Yamal was old enough to make his own decisions.

entries.title: Waterfront goals: Seattle will host a floating World Cup fan zone
entries.description: The Pacific Northwest city is turning its waterfront into a floating football festival complete with fan zones, watch parties and a pitch.

entries.title: Spain's PM Pedro Sánchez 'proud' of Barcelona's Yamal in Palestinian flag row
entries.description: Relations between Spain and Israel have plummeted since the start of the Gaza war, sparked by the Hamas-led attack on Israel on 7 October 2023.

entries.title: Chelsea appoints ex-Real Madrid boss Xabi Alonso as manager on four-year deal
entries.description: Alonso is widely regarded as one of the exciting new managerial talents in world football despite a short, unsuccessful and drama-filled spell with Spanish giants Real Madrid, which ended by mutual consent in January.

entries.title: Iran heads to World Cup training camp in Turkey with no US visas issued
entries.description: Iran’s national football team is flying to Turkey amid fears over visa approvals from the United States, with limited preparation and no top-tier friendly matches scheduled.

entries.title: Member's Only: The secret of Florentino Pérez's Real Madrid power base
entries.description: Money talks. Especially at Real Madrid. If you can't personally guarantee  €187 million and boast 20 years of club membership, there's no way to take over the club, which has allowed its president Florentino Pérez to rule for nearly two decades.

entries.title: Arsenal win the Premier League ending 22-year wait after Manchester City draw with Bournemouth
entries.description: The Gunners have finally secured a Premier League title ending a 22-year wait for the coveted title after Manchester City drew with Bournemouth. The north Londoners can now set their sights on winning their first Champions League final in Budapest, where they face holders Paris Saint-Germain.

entries.title: Ready to take on World Cup giants, Uzbekistan has 'nothing to lose,’ coach Cannavaro tells Euronews
entries.description: In an exclusive interview for Euronews, the Azzurri legend who won the World Cup two decades ago — now coaching Uzbekistan to their historic first appearance —  explains why his team is ready to face the likes of Cristiano Ronaldo's Portugal.

entries.title: North and South Korean women meet in rare football clash as fans cheer both sides
entries.description: Fans in rain ponchos cheered both North and South Korean women’s football teams during a rare inter-Korean match in Suwon, where North Korea’s Naegohyang Women’s FC defeated Suwon FC Women in the AFC Women’s Champions League semifinal.

entries.title: FIFA U-17 World Cup Qatar 2026 draw made as road to Doha begins
entries.description: Argentina face Australia and Denmark, Japan take on Colombia and Serbia, and hosts Qatar open against Panama, Egypt and Greece, as the road to Doha's Khalifa International Stadium and U-17 football glory begins.

entries.title: EuroLeague Final Four: These are European basketball's most successful teams and countries
entries.description: Greek teams have dominated the EuroLeague Final Four in the modern era.

entries.title: Pep Guardiola to leave Manchester City, bringing close to 10-year trophy-laden spell
entries.description: Guardiola, who had a further year left on his City contract, will take charge of his final game against Aston Villa in the Premier League on Sunday.

entries.title: Qatar exports World Cup know-how as 2026 tournament approaches
entries.description: As football fans gear up for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, Qatar is sharing its 2022 experience and knowledge in organising football's top tournament with its North American peers.

entries.title: FIFA faces US probe over World Cup ticket prices less than two weeks before the tournament
entries.description: The attorneys general of New York and New Jersey have issued subpoenas to FIFA as part of a formal investigation into allegedly deceptive ticketing practices ahead of the 2026 World Cup, with fans reporting sky-high prices.

entries.title: How a looming Samsung AI workers’ strike in Korea led to €350,000 bonuses
entries.description: Samsung is only the second company known to strike a profit-sharing agreement with AI workers, according to local reporting.

entries.title: Spain and EU launch new €10 million quantum computer in Barcelona
entries.description: This supercomputer, located at Barcelona's BSC-CNS centre, can combine classical computing with both digital and analogue quantum computing.

entries.title: AI recruitment: How your next job interview could be with a bot
entries.description: Almost 60% of German respondents to a recent survey said they have conducted an interview with AI. Here are some tips for how to ace it.

entries.title: Blue Origin rocket explodes during engine-firing test on launch pad
entries.description: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket exploded during an engine test at Cape Canaveral, with Jeff Bezos acknowledging a “rough day” and saying the company will rebuild and continue testing.

entries.title: AI agents turned to theft, intimidation and collapse in simulated worlds
entries.description: A new experiment suggests that when advanced AI agents are left to run simulated societies without human oversight, rule-breaking, instability and even systemic collapse can emerge rapidly.

entries.title: The Euronews guide to the 2026 World Cup: Groups, fixtures and European kick-off times
entries.description: Taking place from 11 June to 19 July across the US, Canada and Mexico, this summer’s World Cup will be the largest in history – here’s everything that you need to know.

entries.title: A technorealistic approach to AI literacy in Estonian schools
entries.description: As European governments discuss the ethical use of AI in education and plan budgets to facilitate AI literacy among students, the Estonian AI Leap approach stands out as highly pragmatic and thoughtful.

entries.title: João Fonseca knocks out Djokovic in Roland Garros third round
entries.description: The Brazilian marches on after an epic five-hour battle with the Serb, the former world number one. Jannik Sinner is also out of the French Open.

entries.title: Liverpool head coach Arne Slot leaves club with immediate effect
entries.description: Liverpool's ownership said they had "come to the conclusion that change is necessary in order for the club to keep moving forward".

entries.title: Europe’s innovation gap with US and China in focus at Panathēnea 2026
entries.description: Access to capital, risk-taking and building global companies were central themes in Athens at Panathēnea 2026.

entries.title: French police detain hundreds in Paris after violent celebrations of PSG’s Champions League title
entries.description: The French interior ministry said that some 780 persons were questioned nationally, including 283 in Paris alone.

entries.title: Why is Europe falling behind the US on AI adoption at work?
entries.description: A new study shows a clear gap in workplace AI use between the US and Europe - and suggests management structure may be a key reason why.

entries.title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang unveils 'new era of PC' for AI age: Five key takeaways from GTC
entries.description: The headline announcement was a new PC developed in partnership with Microsoft, which Huang called "the biggest reinvention in 40 years.

entries.title: 2026 World Cup shirts: Six strip designs already fit for a museum
entries.description: From Uruguay’s ancestral armour to Yohji Yamamoto’s shirt for Japan, this summer’s World Cup unveils a range of kits to rival the spectacle on the pitch.

entries.title: President Emmanuel Macron announces €93 billion in 'Choose France' investments
entries.description: Since the first "Choose France" in 2018, a year after Macron came to power, more than 230 projects have been announced, representing several thousand jobs, according to the Élysée Palace.

entries.title: AI avatars and smart footballs: Inside FIFA's high-tech 2026 World Cup
entries.description: FIFA is integrating AI-generated player avatars, tracking technology in the balls and digital coaching assistants in this year’s World Cup.

entries.title: AI agents actively ignore EU law to achieve goals, study finds
entries.description: The best-performing AI agent, Anthropic’s Claude Opus, only complied with EU law in 54% of cases, according to a Dutch non-profit research firm.

entries.title: Uber to begin testing autonomous robotaxis in Munich
entries.description: Already part of the cityscape, in San Francisco and Beijing, driverless taxis could soon be carrying passengers in Munich if regulators give their approval.

entries.title: The 2026 World Cup: Billions promised but will the economic boom arrive?
entries.description: With the kick-off less than ten days away, the largest World Cup in history is set to inject tens of billions into the hosting economies. Yet, analysts are estimating that the relative impact will be marginal.

entries.title: World Cup: major absentees, forgotten 'giants' and big returners
entries.description: Once again Italy will be missing from the World Cup but who else won't be playing and which countries are making a long awaited return?

entries.title: New trade secret rules: China says its AI data is none of your business
entries.description: Under new Chinese regulations, any algorithm, dataset or program not publicly disclosed now counts as a trade secret.

entries.title: Giant Lionel Messi statue dismantled in Kolkata
entries.description: A 21-metre statue of Lionel Messi in Kolkata has been removed less than a year after its unveiling due to safety fears over its stability in strong winds.

entries.title: China's highest bridge attracts millions of visitors
entries.description: China's Huajiang Grand Canyon Bridge has cut travel times, expanded 5G access and helped boost tourism and business in rural Guizhou.

entries.title: Italy marks 80 years of the Republic with military parade in Rome
entries.description: Italy celebrated the 80th anniversary of the birth of the Republic with ceremonies, military parades and flypasts in Rome, commemorating the 1946 vote that paved the way for democracy.

entries.title: Czech police charge four in OnlyFans human trafficking case
entries.description: Young women who had just turned 18 were lured into creating erotic photos and videos before signing management contracts with the perpetrators, who promised to grow their careers.

entries.title: Iran World Cup squad to head for Mexico via Spain
entries.description: Iran’s national football team will travel to Spain before heading to its World Cup base in Mexico, despite players still awaiting Mexican and US visas ahead of the 2026 tournament. The 2026 World Cup is being hosted jointly by the United States, Mexico and Canada.

entries.title: UK: Police and protesters clash at Southampton rally over student killing
entries.description: Clashes broke out between protesters and police in Southampton after the release of bodycam footage linked to the fatal stabbing of student Henry Nowak sparked public anger.

entries.title: You can’t miss these: Best World Cup 2026 squad announcement videos
entries.description: From the Beatles to the African savannah, some national federations were inspired and chose even Royals to annouce the final squad lists for the World Cup.

entries.title: French artist JR's giant 'cave' installation in Paris appears damaged
entries.description: Strong winds and heavy rain damaged artist JR’s giant “La Caverne” installation covering Paris’ Pont Neuf bridge, forcing organisers to postpone its public opening scheduled for June 6.

entries.title: ‘If you throw trash, you lose cash’: Tokyo tourist district introduces littering fines
entries.description: Visitors caught dropping rubbish in Tokyo’s popular Shibuya ward will now be fined on the spot as authorities battle the side effects of Japan’s record tourism boom.

entries.title: Microsoft launches its own AI models to take on OpenAI and Anthropic
entries.description: Seven in-house models unveiled at Build 2026 signal Microsoft's push to cut costs and compete directly on the AI frontier as its biggest investees plan to launch record-breaking IPOs.

entries.title: 2026 World Cup fuels tourism boom in North America with Canada in the lead
entries.description: This month’s football World Cup puts North America at the heart of global tourism and is set to trigger an economic boom across the region, with Canada leading the way, ahead of Mexico and the United States, according to the World Travel & Tourism Council.

entries.title: Moment explosion erupts at Russian oil terminal in St. Petersburg after Ukrainian drone attack
entries.description: Black smoke billowed over St. Petersburg after a Ukrainian drone strike sparked a fire at an oil terminal, according to Ukrainian and Russian officials. The attack came days before President Vladimir Putin is due to attend a major economic forum in the city.

entries.title: White House offers to vet AI models before release after Anthropic security scare
entries.description: AI companies will be able to get their models voluntarily checked by Donald Trump's administration a month before their release, according to the order.

entries.title: Want to live off-grid? This remote Scottish island with cabin could be yours for €405,000
entries.description: The rugged private island, complete with a solar-powered cabin, is going under the hammer for less than the average UK house price. Tucked away in Scotland’s remote Summer Isles, it offers a rare chance to own an off-grid escape in one of the country's most spectacular natural settings.

entries.title: Anthropic expands access to Mythos despite warnings of mass cyberattacks
entries.description: Anthropic is expanding access to Mythos, its powerful cybersecurity model, to 150 new partners across more than 15 countries even as the company warns that a successful attack on any of their systems could affect more than 100 million people.

entries.title: Empty stations and cancelled trains as nationwide strike hits Portugal
entries.description: A general strike in Portugal disrupted transport, schools and hospitals as unions protested labour reforms they say weaken workers' rights.

entries.title: Meliá and Iberostar quit Cuba: Trump's ultimatum leaves Spanish hotel groups under pressure
entries.description: Meliá has announced that it is halting operations at 15 Cuban hotels. Iberostar did so on 1 June at 12 hotels, bowing to US sanctions on Gaesa, the military group controlling tourism.

entries.title: Fire tears through New Delhi building, killing at least 18
entries.description: A fire in a New Delhi building killed at least 18 people and injured several others, with rescue teams pulling dozens from the Malviya Nagar block.

entries.title: Taiwan's Asus unveils 'AI City' and Nvidia-powered laptops at Computex
entries.description: Euronews Next spoke to Asus' co-CEO about Nvidia-powered AI laptops and the company's plan to export its 'AI City' model.

entries.title: EU court partially sides with Meta in a blow to the European Commission
entries.description: The EU's General Court has annulled Facebook Marketplace's gatekeeper designation under the Digital Markets Act — but the ruling is largely procedural, as the Commission had already stripped the platform of that status a year earlier.

entries.title: South Summit says 'AI is not a threat' and calls for Europe without '27 different borders'
entries.description: South Summit opens in Madrid with debate centred on AI, as organisers, investors and politicians call for a more competitive Europe that retains talent and startups.

entries.title: South Summit says 'AI is not a threat' and urges a Europe without '27 borders'
entries.description: South Summit opens in Madrid with artificial intelligence at the centre of debate, as organisers, investors and politicians demand a competitive Europe that retains talent and startups.

entries.title: 'Spain has talent; the challenge is funding and solving problems'
entries.description: At the Ibiza Tech Forum 2026, Pilar Carrato explains why scalability, financial discipline and successful exits are vital for Spanish start-ups, offering entrepreneurs practical guidance to turn their ideas into real success.

entries.title: 'Ibiza and Sant Josep are the ideal testbed where innovation meets quality of life'
entries.description: The Pityusic island is hosting the Ibiza Tech Forum 2026 and casting itself as a global tech testbed, using data, AI, cameras and drones to improve services and curb illegal tourist lets.

entries.title: Freedom Ship: Could this giant ‘floating city’ become the world's biggest cruise ship?
entries.description: The proposed Freedom Ship would be unlike anything currently sailing the seas. Designed as a permanent floating city for up to 80,000 people, the mile-long vessel would feature homes, schools, hotels, parks, a hospital and even a sports stadium as it slowly circles the globe.

entries.title: World Cup player statues toppled during protest in Mexico City
entries.description: Teachers in Mexico City toppled football statues during protests over pay and reforms, raising tensions ahead of the World Cup opening next month.

entries.title: Albania: Fourth day of unrest as Trump-linked resort sparks protests
entries.description: On the fourth consecutive day of protests, demonstrators clashed with police in central Tirana on 3 June over a luxury resort project linked to the Trump family.

entries.title: Cuba’s fuel crisis leaves Havana’s streets overflowing with rubbish
entries.description: Cuba is facing a mounting waste management crisis as fuel shortages continue to disrupt rubbish collection across Havana, the country's capital.

entries.title: France: Protesters rally outside CNews TV over alleged Kremlin influence
entries.description: France witnessed renewed debate over foreign influence in the media on 3 June as dozens of protesters gathered outside CNews headquarters in Paris to denounce Ksenia Fedorova, the former head of RT France.

entries.title: Revealed: The most beautiful restaurants in Europe for 2026
entries.description: The Prix Versailles architecture and design awards have revealed the World’s Most Beautiful Restaurants for 2026. Five European venues made the prestigious list, celebrating restaurants where striking interiors are every bit as memorable as the food.

entries.title: New York mayor Mamdani and Bastian Schweinsteiger unveil World Cup trophy
entries.description: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani and former Germany captain Bastian Schweinsteiger welcomed the World Cup trophy to New York as part of FIFA's official Trophy Tour ahead of the 2026 tournament.

entries.title: This Finnish ski resort is launching a summer-long treasure hunt for a €20,000 gold bar
entries.description: Visitors will have longer to search in the daylight as Levi experiences the midnight sun from late May to the end of July.

entries.title: Amazon pledges €10 billion for Europe with 25,000 new jobs and warehouse robots
entries.description: Amazon is making one of its biggest new bets on Europe, announcing more than €10 billion in investments to expand and modernise its warehouse and delivery network across the continent over the coming years.

entries.title: From AI robot baristas to military drones: The weird and wonderful tech at Computex 2026
entries.description: Euronews Next spotlights the most innovative and intriguing tech at the conference in Taiwan.

entries.title: France unveils 33 ‘palaces’ for 2026 as six new hotels join elite list
entries.description: The latest collection of palace hotels have been revealed, with six properties joining France’s most exclusive hospitality club.

entries.title: Travel back to the time of the great Arctic expeditions with a stay in this 19th-century style cabin
entries.description: HX Expeditions is marking the 130th anniversary of its landmark expedition cruise by unveiling the 1896 Cabin.

entries.title: Love night trains? This new map shows all the sleeper services running across Europe in 2026
entries.description: The 2026 edition shows five new connections already running or lauching this year.

entries.title: 10 iconic moments that shaped World Cup history
entries.description: The 2026 World Cup kicks off next week in Mexico City, where joint hosts Mexico play South Africa.

entries.title: Iran war, strikes, EES: Why the number of people flying in Europe is dropping
entries.description: An industry group says that while marginal, the decline “marks a significant milestone” for Europe’s aviation sector.

entries.title: Gaza: Families mourn victims after overnight Israeli strikes that killed at least nine
entries.description: Families gathered in Gaza City to mourn relatives killed on 4 June after overnight Israeli airstrikes in Gaza City, according to local hospitals.

entries.title: Gaza city residents survey destruction after overnight Israeli airstrike
entries.description: Gaza residents surveyed extensive damage on 4 June after an overnight Israeli airstrike hit an apartment in the Sheikh Radwan neighbourhood of Gaza City.

entries.title: The dark side of the 2026 World Cup: Record-breaking emissions and thousands of flights
entries.description: A report warns that the tournament in the US, Canada and Mexico could produce twice the emissions of previous editions.

entries.title: Why are European governments reevaluating their agreements with US defence tech contractor Palantir?
entries.description: Critics have flagged Palantir for their links to militaries involved in ongoing conflicts and ICE immigration enforcement in the United States.

entries.title: Africa’s hottest new safari lodges are redefining luxury travel in the wild
entries.description: From helicopter arrivals in Zambia to design-led lodges in Tanzania and ultra-luxury retreats in Botswana, a new wave of safari openings across Africa is transforming the classic game drive holiday into something far more immersive, stylish and experience-led.

entries.title: Denmark probes death of humpback whale that captivated Germany
entries.description: Denmark has launched a post-mortem examination on Timmy, a humpback whale whose struggle to return to the sea captured public attention in Germany and beyond. Scientists hope to determine why the animal died weeks after being returned to open waters.

entries.title: Thousands join Jerusalem pride parade under tight security
entries.description: Thousands joined Jerusalem Pride parade on 4 June, under heavy security, marching for equality and acceptance in a deeply conservative, religiously significant city.

entries.title: Venezuela's Dancing Devils revive centuries-old Corpus Christi rite
entries.description: Children making their First Communion were accompanied by masked "Dancing Devils" in Tarmas, Venezuela. The centuries-old Corpus Christi celebration symbolises the triumph of good over evil and remains a key cultural tradition.

entries.title: All you need is... an official date: Inaugural Global Beatles Day announced
entries.description: Unofficially celebrated since 2009, The Beatles and Apple Corps Ltd. have announced that the first official Global Beatles Day will take place later this month. It will include online and in-person events around the world...

entries.title: 'Jumanji' and 'Top Gun: Maverick' actor James Handy stabbed to death aged 81
entries.description: Handy enjoyed a career spanning five decades, with notable roles including the exterminator in Jumanji, Detective Alan Cross in The X-Files, and bartender Jimmy in Top Gun: Maverick.

entries.title: Trump ‘oil painting’ football image goes viral and sparks Pride Month jokes
entries.description: Timing is everything, and an image reportedly posted by Donald Trump on his Truth Social platform has caused a stir, considering June is Pride Month. An inadvertent celebration?

entries.title: Anthropic calls for ‘brake pedal’ before AI develops itself without human oversight
entries.description: Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark said AI agents might soon be able to build and train models themselves and, if that happens, humans could lose control over AI systems.

entries.title: Jack White’s panned exhibition: Which musicians found success in fine art?
entries.description: Jack White’s debut exhibition has divided opinion. Here’s how other music icons fared in the court of public opinion and the eyes of critics.

entries.title: 'Moved to tears': Winners of the 2026 World Food Photography Awards revealed
entries.description: From Soviet spas to sacred rivers, this World Food Photography Awards offer a glimpse into the many ways food influences our lives around the globe.

entries.title: Romania: TV reporter flees live on air after drone explosion in Constanța
entries.description: A live television report from Romania's Black Sea port of Constanța was abruptly interrupted on 5 June after a maritime drone exploded nearby, heightening concerns over the regional impact of the war in Ukraine.

entries.title: Lebanon: UN clears debris in Dibbine after Israeli withdrawal
entries.description: UN peacekeepers and heavy machinery entered the southern Lebanese village of Dibbine on 5 June to clear debris left by recent fighting.

entries.title: Counter-drone technology showcased as Europe develops plans for 'drone wall'
entries.description: Drone makers and defence firms showcased the latest drone detection and counter-drone technologies at a major industry event in Denmark, as European countries step up efforts to address growing security concerns linked to UAV incursions and modern warfare.

entries.title: Bayeux Tapestry set to go on show at British Museum
entries.description: The medieval masterpiece will be displayed at the London museum from September 2026 to July 2027 to mark the 1,000th anniversary of William the Conqueror.

entries.title: Euronews Culture's Film of the Week: 'Backrooms' - Liminal space horror you'll want to get lost in
entries.description: Released last week in the US and UK, this year’s breakout horror hit finally heads to European cinemas. It was worth the wait.

entries.title: Germany: Bavarian lake procession marks centuries-old boat tradition for Corpus Christi
entries.description: Hundreds of worshippers gathered in Bavaria on 4 June for Seehausen's traditional Corpus Christi lake procession. The event, held annually since 1935, combines religious devotion with local heritage on the waters of Staffelsee.

entries.title: Tributes paid to MOBO Awards founder and cultural pioneer Kanya King, dead at 57
entries.description: MOBO Awards founder Kanya King championed Black musicians' contribution to British culture and funded the first Music Of Black Origin awards in 1996. Heartfelt tributes have been posted, remembering her as a "visionary" and "a pioneer" who "changed the face of culture and music.”

entries.title: ‘None of us could have predicted this’: Scottish island closes on Sundays after visitor surge
entries.description: After featuring in a BBC show, Ulva received “unprecedented interest” from holidaymakers, according to local ferry operators.

entries.title: Portugal to deploy over 300 more police officers at airports this summer to curb queues
entries.description: The measure comes in the wake of bottlenecks recently reported at border control in some Portuguese airports, with Lisbon particularly affected.

entries.title: Qatari artist designs Pierre Gasly's helmet for Canadian Grand Prix
entries.description: The design, created through the 2026 Year of Culture partnership involving Qatar, Canada and Mexico, combines symbols from both Qatar and Canada.

entries.title: Patagonia vs Pattie Gonia: Why is the clothing giant suing the famous drag queen?
entries.description: American clothing company Patagonia has filed a trademark infringement lawsuit against drag queen and environmental activist Pattie Gonia, prompting a public dispute over branding, activism, and intellectual property rights.

entries.title: Vancouver: A travel guide to the 2026 World Cup host city
entries.description: BC Place in downtown Vancouver will host seven World Cup fixtures – including two of Canada's group games.

entries.title: The Baltic states need more drone-detection radars. Europe's defence bottlenecks may slow them down
entries.description: The Baltic States, like the rest of Europe, are grappling with equipment shortages for the key technologies that will make it easier to respond to drone incursions, experts say.

entries.title: Philadelphia: A travel guide to the 2026 World Cup host city
entries.description: Philadelphia will host five World Cup group games as well as a Round of 16 clash on 4 July – exactly 250 years after the Declaration of Independence was signed in the historic city.

entries.title: Spain welcomes Pope Leo XIV on landmark six-day tour: key events and highlights
entries.description: Millions are expected to attend events across Spain as Leo XIV combines religious celebrations with high-profile political meetings.

entries.title: Lazio Maremma cowboys: tradition and innovation at Canale Monterano's Riarto
entries.description: For centuries, the buttero was central to livestock and rural life in the Maremma between Lazio and Tuscany. Though the trade is fading, new horsemen keep the region's history and identity alive.

entries.title: 'Mehrzweckeier': dig at Merz for youth word of the year? Voting under way
entries.description: 'Mehrzweckeier': youth word of the year as a protest? Citizens can now vote on it officially. What began as criticism of conscription reform is now aimed at Friedrich Merz. Insult or free speech?

entries.title: Dua Lipa and Callum Turner celebrate with guests at Sicily wedding party
entries.description: Newlyweds Dua Lipa and Callum Turner were seen greeting guests and celebrating at a wedding party in Palermo, Sicily, as part of a weekend of festivities following their recent London wedding.

entries.title: Tennis: Mirra Andreeva wins women's Roland Garros final
entries.description: The world No. 8 duly brushed aside Polish qualifier Maja Chwalinska, winning in straight sets, 6-3 6-2.  Mirra Andreeva - nineteen years old - is the youngest winner since Monica Seles

entries.title: NASA aircraft breaks sound barrier in first supersonic test flight
entries.description: The milestone comes after a busy testing period for NASA. Since the first flight on 28 October 2025, the team has completed 16 flights in the last 90 days

entries.title: Toronto: A travel guide to the 2026 World Cup host city
entries.description: Canada's largest city will host six World Cup matches, including the co-host nation’s opening fixture.

entries.title: Guadalajara: A travel guide to the 2026 World Cup host city
entries.description: Mexico's second-largest city is hosting its third men's World Cup after previously staging matches in 1970 and 1986.

entries.title: Thousands join record-breaking human wave attempt in Mexico City ahead of World Cup
entries.description: Thousands of people gathered in Mexico City to attempt a Guinness World Record for the world's largest human wave, days before the start of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

entries.title: 13 floors, 1,240 rooms: largest Baltic Sea hotel opens – but there is a catch
entries.description: Thirteen floors and 1,240 rooms: a mega-hotel on the Baltic Sea coast is about to open – but this is where the real snag lies.

entries.title: Inside Amazon’s busiest European warehouse, where robots, lasers and humans deliver the future
entries.description: Amazon says robots will make work safer and deliveries faster. Euronews Next saw them in action.

entries.title: Hitler's 70 km ghost motorway: Germany's longest abandoned autobahn
entries.description: Route 46 is Germany’s longest abandoned motorway project. It owes less to transport efficiency than to Nazi-era construction ideology and propaganda.

entries.title: Cannes winner Jafar Panahi faces prison and travel ban from Iran
entries.description: On Sunday, Mostafa Nili, lawyer for Jafar Panahi, announced that a Tehran Revolutionary Court has rejected the objections and fully upheld the in-absentia verdict against the prominent Iranian filmmaker.

entries.title: 2026 World Cup: five key facts about FIFA's biggest ever tournament
entries.description: The 2026 World Cup will be the first with 48 teams, 104 matches and three host nations. With record figures, extreme heat risks and Super Bowl-style spectacle, the North American tournament promises to break the mould.

entries.title: German Zverev wins his first Grand Slam, beating Italian Cobolli
entries.description: Zverev’s serve remained on point at crucial moments, and he broke Cobolli at 4–5 in the fourth set to force the tiebreak. He then dominated the fifth set with two breaks and consolidated both.

entries.title: Danish footballer Christian Eriksen 'conscious' after collapsing on field
entries.description: The former Tottenham and Manchester United midfielder went down on the pitch in Odense, Denmark’s third-largest city. According to reports, he remains conscious, and the match has been abandoned

entries.title: Travelling to Belgium? An expert shares their top tips for visitors
entries.description: Belgium expert Angela Dansby reveals her top travel tips for the country, including attractions to see and the food and drink to try.

entries.title: Pope Leo XIV meets Antonio Banderas, watches flamenco performance in Madrid
entries.description: Pope Leo XIV attended a cultural event in Madrid featuring performances, speeches and appearances by leading figures from the worlds of arts, culture and sport.

entries.title: Aliens, Pan-Africanism and Olivia Rodrigo: What to see, do or hear this week in Europe
entries.description: Steven Spielberg's 'Disclosure Day' beams into the box office while Olivia Rodrigo sings about love making us crazy. Here's what to look out for this week.

entries.title: The world's most travelled people gather in Portugal for annual meet-up
entries.description: In Portugal, the Most Traveled People (MTP) community brought together around 200 die-hard travellers, half of whom have already visited every country in the world recognised by the UN.

entries.title: ‘He was the best of us’: Heartfelt tributes paid to Buffy star Anthony Head, dead at 72
entries.description: Best known for his role as Giles on the cult TV show Buffy The Vampire Slayer, Anthony Head died at the age of 72. Over the weekend, his co-stars and friends paid heartfelt tributes to a performer who will be sorely missed.

entries.title: Iranians protest outside World Cup stadium ahead of Iran match
entries.description: More than 200 members of the Iranian diaspora gathered outside Los Angeles Stadium on Sunday, waving Iran's pre-revolutionary "Lion and Sun" flags and protesting against the Iranian government ahead of Iran's first match of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

entries.title: Patrick Bruel in police custody over sexual assault allegations
