entries.title: Iran, tech and Trump to top Macron's G7 summit
entries.description: Heads of state from the G7 are bracing themselves for the arrival of US President Donald Trump at the first day of a major summit in Évian-les-Bains, with an agenda bogged down in geopolitics, AI, and the news of a potential breakthrough in the war in Iran.

entries.title: Police mistakenly shoot and kill Australian child in eastern Pakistan, officials say
entries.description: Australian media reported the family, from the western city of Perth, were visiting relatives in Pakistan when the incident took place.

entries.title: Norway crown princess' son sentenced to four years in prison on two counts of rape
entries.description: Marius Borg Høiby, 29, is the eldest son of Crown Princess Mette-Marit from a previous relationship and the stepson of the heir to the throne, Crown Prince Haakon.

entries.title: Does the EU have a drug problem? Take our poll
entries.description: The European Council has approved its first ever drug framework to reduce drug use and drug related crimes in the EU.

entries.title: Watch: Washington, Anthropic, and the AI kill switch—can Europe actually build tech independence?
entries.description: Washington has just reminded Europe who really holds the power in the age of artificial intelligence. For Europe, this is a long-overdue wake-up call. But will it actually wake up?

entries.title: British PM Keir Starmer announces social media ban for under-16-year-olds
entries.description: Australia, Canada, Brazil and Indonesia have introduced legislation or announced age-based restrictions or requirements for children’s access to social media.

entries.title: EU leaders welcome US-Iran deal to end war as Macron says mission to aid Hormuz reopening 'ready'
entries.description: The US and Iran said they had reached a deal to end the war on all fronts, including Lebanon, and reopen the vital Strait of Hormuz, though they offered few details about Tehran's nuclear programme.

entries.title: ‘Gwynocide’: Gwyneth Paltrow facing backlash for 'tone deaf' luxury Israel real estate advert
entries.description: The Oscar-winning actress has appeared in an advert for luxury penthouses in Israel. The backlash has been intense, with one celebrity asking whether Paltrow is “actually a really, really nasty person” or just “stupid”.

entries.title: Switzerland: Clashes in Geneva as 20,000 protest G7 summit and global policies
entries.description: Clashes broke out in Geneva on 14 June after a large demonstration held ahead of the G7 summit in nearby Évian-les-Bains, France, turned violent near the United Nations district.

entries.title: World Naked Bike Ride returns to streets of London in the UK
entries.description: Hundreds of cyclists took part in the World Naked Bike Ride in London on 14 June. The annual event combined humour, environmental messages, and calls for greater safety on the roads.

entries.title: From the Caspian to the Balkans: What Air Serbia’s Baku-Belgrade route is really like
entries.description: Air Serbia’s new Baku to Belgrade route is offering travellers a smoother connection between the Caucasus, the Balkans and wider Europe.

entries.title: Berlin musician turns Germany football opener into musical spectacle
entries.description: Germany's opening match of the 2026 FIFA World Cup became an unusual cultural event on 14 June in Berlin, where composer Stephan Graf von Bothmer performed a live musical score as the national team faced Curaçao.

entries.title: Newsletter: The G7's moment of reckoning
entries.description: Also in this newsletter: Ukraine and Moldova enter first phase of talks on joining the European Union; and Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy prepares to join G7 leaders after Russian strikes kill nine and damage landmark cathedral overnight.

entries.title: Cocaine capitals: Where is use rising the most in Europe?
entries.description: Spain's Barcelona, Lleida and Santiago de Compostela report the highest increase in cocaine wastewater residues, with other clusters also expanding in Denmark and Slovenia.

entries.title: Visit the cats of Rome on this new running tour around the Eternal City
entries.description: You’ll visit sights like the Colosseum and Pyramid of Cestius, passing by feline colonies along the way.

entries.title: MEPs pressure EU institutions to sanction Irish alumina sales to Russia
entries.description: A joint letter warns that exported material is directly feeding Moscow's war machine, and calls for a public-private plan to redirect supply to European buyers.

entries.title: Starbucks to close outlets across Korea for history lesson after 'Tank Day' ad fiasco
entries.description: The day of the reusable cup promotion coincided with the anniversary of the Gwangju uprising in which 165 civilians were killed, though many believe the real figure to be much higher.

entries.title: G7 summit: France ready to deploy ships to Strait of Hormuz, Macron says
entries.description: French President Emmanuel Macron is hosting his last G7 summit before leaving office next year. High on the agenda are Russia's war on Ukraine, the conflict in the Middle East, Chinese competition, AI and child safety online.

entries.title: ‘Snowflake’: Donald Trump's Kennedy Center tarp cover-up sparks criticism
entries.description: Donald Trump’s name has been removed from the facade of the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts. But striped tarps still cover the cultural center, leading to frustration and several accusing Trump of having a “fragile ego”.

entries.title: Greece, Spain and Italy drive surge in summer 2026 travel demand
entries.description: New data shows that Southern Europe is the fastest-growing tourism region this summer, as travellers look towards destinations that feel ‘closer, more familiar and more accessible’, amid geopolitical uncertainty and aviation disruption.

entries.title: Ukraine: Kyiv under fire again as russian strikes send residents into shelters
entries.description: Ukraine's capital came under a major missile attack early on 15 June, with air defence systems intercepting projectiles above the city. Authorities reported injuries as residents sought refuge in shelters and metro stations.

entries.title: At least 2,300 killed this year in Haiti gang violence, UN human rights chief says
entries.description: Haiti has been plagued by worsening gang violence since the 2021 assassination of its then-President Jovenel Moïse.

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | June 15th, 2026 – Midday
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 15th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: Baby boom after the summer fairytale: does football joy raise birth rates?
entries.description: If an entire country is celebrating, does that mean more babies are born nine months later? Following the 2006 and 2014 World Cups, this theory persisted. A look at the statistics, maternity wards and research reveals what truth there really is to it.

entries.title: Russian attack sets fire to a UNESCO-listed monastery in Kyiv
entries.description: A major Russian attack sparked a fire at a Kyiv monastery, as missiles and drones hit multiple sites across the Ukrainian capital.

entries.title: Rare masterpieces from former Tottenham FC owner's collection head to auction in London
entries.description: The collection, belonging to billionaire and former Tottenham Hotspur owner Joe Lewis, features masterpieces by Modigliani, Klimt, Picasso and Freud.

entries.title: Iran declares victory and Israel holds its ground as US framework deal faces first test
entries.description: Tehran declared the US "was forced to sign" a surrender while Iran's military said enemies had "no option but to accept defeat," as Israel said IDF forces would remain in Lebanon, Syria and Gaza "for an unlimited period".

entries.title: Proscribing pro-Palestinian group as 'terrorist' was lawful, UK court says
entries.description: Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper had defended the proscription, arguing supporters of the group were unaware of the "full nature" of the organisation.

entries.title: Air travellers to enjoy free cabin luggage and keep delay compensation after decade-long talks
entries.description: Airlines argue that if the sector is required to comply with stricter compensation and financial rules, ticket prices will inevitably go up.

entries.title: Want to book a life-changing holiday? Then travel to these destinations
entries.description: Searching for more than a beach break? These are the places that travellers say transformed their lives when they returned home.

entries.title: Trial opens for Swedish army consultant accused of attempted espionage for Russia
entries.description: The trial, scheduled to last three days, is being held largely behind closed doors, given the sensitive nature of the information involved.

entries.title: Robert De Niro has a four-word cinematic reference for Donald Trump
entries.description: Safe to say that screen legend Robert De Niro did not send Donald Trump a birthday card for his 80th... At a New York event last night, the actor used his "own free speech to respond" to some of Trump's recent declarations. He also made a movie reference which was chanted by the crowd.

entries.title: The world’s most beautiful airports 2026 list has been revealed – and only one is in Europe
entries.description: Prix Versailles has unveiled its list of the seven most beautiful airports in the world for 2026.

entries.title: Do the World Cup's pricey tickets reflect US market rates and stop scalpers, as FIFA says?
entries.description: FIFA says World Cup ticket prices reflect US market rates and help prevent scalpers from profiting. But experts and consumer groups say the comparison with US sports leaves out important context.

entries.title: Delays to UK's Houses of Parliament renovation could add £420 million to bill
entries.description: Plans to restore the Victorian neo-Gothic edifice, which is part of a UNESCO World Heritage Site featuring Westminster Abbey, stretch back more than a decade.

entries.title: US businessman detained in Myanmar over alleged Chamber of Commerce fraud
entries.description: A former US Marine and ex-AmCham Myanmar president was detained at Yangon airport Thursday after the chamber uncovered $300,000 in allegedly unauthorised payments linked to former board members.

entries.title: Spanish police conclude initials 'P.S.' in diary of former party member refer to PM Pedro Sánchez
entries.description: A Civil Guard report identifies the initials "P.S." with the prime minister and names Santos Cerdán as the driving force behind efforts to derail the case against Sánchez's brother.

entries.title: Lavender Provence farmers forced to raise laying hens to keep their activity going
entries.description: The emblem of Provence, which attracts thousands of tourists every year, isn't as profitable as before. Lavender has become a burden for many farmers who are operating at a loss and choose to diversify to survive.

entries.title: Voices of Pride: European celebrities advocating for LGBTQIA+ rights
entries.description: Pride Month, celebrated every June, shines a spotlight on LGBTQIA+ people, their achievements, and the challenges they continue to face. While support for the community often becomes more visible during this time of year, many public figures advocate for LGBTQIA+ rights year-round.

entries.title: Ukraine and Moldova press ahead with bid to join the EU on enlargement ‘Mega Monday’
entries.description: In what’s been hailed by the European Union’s enlargement chief as ‘Mega Monday’, Ukraine and Moldova are breaking a two-year political deadlock by formally kicking off the first phase of negotiations on joining the bloc.

entries.title: Czech government scraps licence fees for public media in move critics call threat to press freedom
entries.description: The right-wing populist coalition of Prime Minister Andrej Babiš has approved a bill replacing the traditional funding model with direct state financing, triggering strikes and protests.

entries.title: From energy corridors to AI: Can the Turkic world become a new economic force?
entries.description: Turkic states met in Shusha this weekend to boost competition law, digital cooperation and trade, aiming to align antitrust rules and strengthen regional connectivity.

entries.title: Moscow strike on Kyiv-Pechersk Lavra 'one of Russia’s most serious crimes against Christian culture'
entries.description: The Kyiv-Pechersk Lavra, one of the most important historic and religious symbols of Ukraine, has been hit in Moscow's drone and missile attack overnight on Monday, in what President Zelenskyy has called “one of Russia’s most serious crimes against Christian culture to date.”

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | June 15th, 2026 – Evening
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 15th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: Two men convicted of arson plot targeting UK prime minister's properties
entries.description: Two men face sentencing Friday after being found guilty of torching properties linked to the prime minister, in a case that has raised questions about foreign interference in British public life.

entries.title: Albania assures EU its Kushner-linked tourism project will meet environmental standards
entries.description: Albania has promised that a €1.4bn project linked to Donald Trump's son-in-law will meet EU environmental standards, Enlargement Commissioner Marta Kos said on Monday.

entries.title: EU fails to reach agreement on sanctioning Israel's Ben Gvir despite member state pressure
entries.description: The bloc's foreign policy chief confirmed that discussions on restricting trade with illegal West Bank settlements will also continue ahead of July's ministerial meeting.

entries.title: 'Spider-Man of Yemen' dies after fall into volcanic crater
entries.description: A Yemeni adventurer known online as the “Spider-Man of Yemen” died after falling into a volcanic crater while attempting a climb without safety equipment.

entries.title: Spain record surprising goalless draw against Cape Verde
entries.description: Debutants Cape Verde, a nation of over 500,000 people, held out against Luis de la Fuente’s star-studded team, playing defensively and relying on few counterattacks that nearly caused an upset for Spain in the 90th.

entries.title: Meet Merlin: The duck that became the World Cup's viral mascot
entries.description: A duck named Merlin has become an unlikely World Cup sensation after videos of him wearing a Mexico jersey and joining celebrations in Mexico City spread across social media.

entries.title: 21-year-old woman dies after being launched off bridge without safety rope
entries.description: Viral videos of the tragedy shared online show the anguish of onlookers when they realised the lady was launched off the Ponte do Esqueleto bridge in Brazil without the required safety rope. “She’s got no rope,” one witness to the accident is heard saying.

entries.title: B-52 bomber crashes at Edwards Air Force Base in California
entries.description: A B-52 Stratofortress went down at around 11:20 a.m. local time, with emergency crews scrambled to the scene. No casualty information has been released. The aircraft typically carries a crew of five.

entries.title: Spain fails to impress in World Cup opener against Cape Verde as Sweden steamroll 5-1 past Tunisia
entries.description: Spain, considered by many as one of the favourites to win the title, failed to impress in their opening match against Cape Verde, which ended in a nil-nil draw, as La Roja star Lamine Yamal remains sidelined with injury.

entries.title: Scientists drill into Spain's only meteorite crater for clues to Mars
entries.description: Researchers are drilling 500 metres into Spain's only internationally recognised meteorite crater, hoping to learn more about an ancient impact event and gather insights that could help scientists understand Mars.

entries.title: Trump appears hesitant about ally-proposed plan to demine Strait of Hormuz for commercial traffic
entries.description: Trump appeared hesitant to accept the European proposal, stressing that the strategic waterway will become open and safe thanks to his tentative deal to end the war with Iran, but remained open to the idea of some naval presence to ensure water safety for commercial passage.

entries.title: Spain, Italy and France from the sea: inside one of the world's most popular cruises
entries.description: The western Mediterranean is one of the world’s most popular cruise routes, drawing thousands who visit three top European destinations in a single trip without ever packing or unpacking.

entries.title: 6.7 magnitude earthquake strikes off of Indonesia, US Geological Survey says
entries.description: Authorities have urged the public to heed official advice and instructions and exercise caution, warning that aftershocks can last hours.

entries.title: World Cup 2026: Six million fans, three countries and a team racing to stop outbreaks
entries.description: As six million fans travel across North America for the 2026 World Cup, health authorities are on alert for any sign of an infectious disease outbreak.

entries.title: ‘AI is the key to global power status’: Inside China's race to militarise artificial intelligence
entries.description: The true extent of China's military AI remains unclear, despite public displays of autonomous drones, AI-powered naval weapons and "robot dogs," experts told Euronews.

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | June 16th, 2026 – Morning
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 16th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: Book a ‘Chef’s Table’ in business class: United upgrades dining on select long-haul flights
entries.description: Ever wondered what happens when a streaming hit and an airline unite? US carrier United is collaborating with Netflix’s Chef’s Table to bring 30 new chef-curated dishes to its Polaris international business class.

entries.title: Europe Today: Inside the G7 Summit in Évian
entries.description: Global leaders are gathering in the French spa town of Évian les Bains for the second day of the G7. US President Donald Trump told allies that he expects the Strait of Hormuz to be “fully open” on Friday, where the framework deal to end the war is expected to be formalised in Switzerland.

entries.title: Bonnie Tyler out of induced coma but still in serious condition
entries.description: The 75-year-old Welsh singer has been in intensive care at Faro Hospital since early May following emergency surgery for a perforated bowel.

entries.title: G7 summit: Trump asks Netanyahu to be 'more responsible' in Lebanon
entries.description: The second day of the G7 summit in Évian, France, is underway. The topics on the agenda include Russia's war on Ukraine, the situation in the Middle East and international partnerships.

entries.title: Iran 'played Trump like a violin' in deal, former national security adviser Bolton tells Euronews
entries.description: Bolton tells Euronews the US sacrificed its strategic leverage as Trump prioritised lowering oil prices, while Iran walks away with the deal it wanted.

entries.title: Eight killed in US Air Force B-52 bomber crash in California
entries.description: Eight people were killed when a B-52 bomber crashed and burst into flames shortly after takeoff from Edwards Air Force Base in Southern California. Officials said the aircraft was on a routine test mission and the cause remains under investigation.

entries.title: Protesting and cheering as Iran's World Cup match divides diaspora
entries.description: Iran's opening World Cup match drew both supporters and protesters outside the stadium near Los Angeles, highlighting divisions within the Iranian-American community.

entries.title: €31bn drug trade, 7,600 deaths: How the EU plans to tackle the drug crisis
entries.description: 29 million Europeans use drugs every year, and the market has an estimated €31 billion value. The European Council has released a new framework to completely change how the EU handles this enormous problem, from enforcement to public health.

entries.title: WATCH: Bolton says Trump played like violin by Iran
entries.description: Iran outmanoeuvred US President Donald Trump "like a violin" in negotiations, walking away with far better terms after sensing his desperation for a deal to end the war, former National Security Adviser John Bolton told Euronews.

entries.title: A cave for a crowd: French artist JR opens 'Caverne' art work on Pont Neuf bridge in Paris
entries.description: Open until 28 June, JR’s ephemeral art installation, by the master of XXL photography, immerses Parisian passers-by in a vast cave.

entries.title: Crowds flock to JR's giant cave installation on Paris' Pont Neuf
entries.description: Visitors queued to explore French artist JR's new immersive installation, which transforms Paris' oldest bridge, the Pont Neuf, into a giant cave open around the clock.

entries.title: Europe's top listening hotels: Check in for music, DJs and vinyl
entries.description: From vinyl-fuelled cocktail lounges in Athens and London to a luxury listening suite in Lake Como, European hotels are turning sound into the main attraction, blending analogue music, DJ culture and design-led hospitality into immersive stays.

entries.title: Fact check: Is the EU fining member states that don't accept migrants?
entries.description: A major reform of the EU’s migration and asylum system has recently entered into force, prompting political figures in France to criticise one of its key provisions. However, their claims are misleading.

entries.title: Seaplanes: Greece-Albania link takes off, with more international routes planned
entries.description: Hellenic Seaplanes is expanding its network, introducing new routes that will connect the city of Ioannina with Vlorë and Pogradec in neighbouring Albania.

entries.title: The best FIFA World Cup 2026 aircraft liveries
entries.description: National carriers have sent their teams to the world’s biggest stage in style aboard special themed planes.

entries.title: Hundreds of cats destined for slaughter rescued in Vietnam as police dismantle theft ring
entries.description: Vietnamese police arrested nine people and rescued more than 400 live cats destined for slaughter, reuniting dozens with their owners. Around 100 of the animals later died from their ordeal.

entries.title: Europe’s most welcoming countries and cities for expats
entries.description: A new report looks at factors including foreign-born employment rates, safety, and overall expat experience.

entries.title: Rare first edition of ‘Wuthering Heights’ (complete with spelling mistakes) to go up for auction
entries.description: It’s the first copy of the Gothic novel in the publisher’s original cloth binding to be auctioned since 1908.

entries.title: Ships start to trickle through Strait of Hormuz, but who will run it still in doubt
entries.description: Trump said the strait would be "completely open" by Friday, but key details over who will manage the crucial waterway remain unresolved. Meanwhile, Iran has made further claims that it will control Hormuz in the future based on its interpretation of the framework deal with the US.

entries.title: Anish Kapoor brings darkness, mirrors and the 'sublime' to London’s Hayward Gallery
entries.description: A major new exhibition showcasing the work of Turner Prize-winner Anish Kapoor has opened at London’s Hayward Gallery, bringing together blood-red suspended sculptures, distorted mirror installations and void-like forms that warp perception and space.

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | June 16th, 2026 – Midday
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 16th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: Moscow oil refinery ablaze after retaliatory Ukrainian drone strike
entries.description: Kyiv claimed the attack as retaliation for Russia's intensifying aerial campaign, a day after drones damaged a UNESCO-listed monastery complex in the capital.

entries.title: Humanoid robots join models at fashion show in Seoul
entries.description: Humanoid robots wearing designer outfits shared the runway with human models at a futuristic fashion show in Seoul, highlighting advances in robotics and AI.

entries.title: Education: Where in the EU has the most early school dropouts?
entries.description: The number of young Europeans leaving education early is falling, but geography and employment prospects still shape the picture. How do EU countries compare with each other?

entries.title: MEPs approve EU-US trade deal despite Trump’s new trade war threats
entries.description: The agreement removes EU tariffs on US goods while accepting a 15 percent levy in the other direction.

entries.title: Watch: German chancellor surprises Trump with 'Trump 47' football jersey at G7
entries.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz presented U.S. President Donald Trump with a personalized football jersey during the G7 summit in France.

entries.title: Trump tells Netanyahu 'be responsible with Lebanon, let Syria handle Hezbollah' at G7
entries.description: Trump questions Netanyahu's methods, says Beirut strike was excessive, and points to Syria’s President as the most “capable” of countering Hezbollah.

entries.title: Russian Tu-22M3 bomber crashes during training flight
entries.description: Russian Tu-22M3 strategic bomber has crashed in the Irkutsk region during a training flight, amid reports of engine failure. The Defence Ministry said it was not carrying weapons and the crew ejected safely. Four people were taken to hospital.

entries.title: London West End's Shaftesbury Theatre to be renamed to honour Judi Dench
entries.description: The name change of one of the West End’s most iconic stages celebrates one of the greatest British performers. Dame Judi Dench has been left “truly overwhelmed” by the news.

entries.title: Who is Pico Lopes, the unlikely Cape Verde World Cup hero recruited through LinkedIn?
entries.description: The national team's call came from 4,500 km away across the Ocean and not in the way most people would expect.

entries.title: Sinkevičius says he will lead reshuffled Lithuanian government following coalition turmoil
entries.description: The three-party coalition must still sign an agreement formalising the new alliance, expected to take place on 18 June, which will then be approved by the country's president.

entries.title: UN says 2.4m refugees will need resettling in 2027 and warns of shortage of options
entries.description: Afghan refugees are the largest group in need of resettlement, followed by people from South Sudan, Sudan, Syria and Rohingya refugees from Myanmar, living in vast camps in Bangladesh.

entries.title: Beyond 'Disclosure Day': Our picks of Steven Spielberg’s most underrated movies
entries.description: Steven Spielberg's 'Disclosure Day' has a strong box office opening despite mixed reviews. Felt alienated by it or looking to revisit / discover some of the director's filmography? The Euronews Culture team shares their picks of Spielberg’s underrated gems.

entries.title: Kyiv seeks license to produce Patriot systems for Ukraine and Europe
entries.description: With Russia deliberately targeting Ukraine’s energy system each winter, Volodymyr Zelenskyy said at the G7 summit that he wants to bring the war to an end before the next cold season. Kyiv now holds a stronger position both on the battlefield and in its strikes deep in Russia.

entries.title: European Parliament lifts immunity of Italian MEP embroiled in Huawei corruption scandal
entries.description: The European Parliament has voted to lift the immunity of Italian MEP Fulvio Martusciello, who is under investigation in an alleged scandal of corruption and illicit lobbying practices by tech company Huawei.

entries.title: Russian artist and Putin critic Semyon Skrepetsky shot dead in Poland, officials say
entries.description: Skrepetsky was known for his sometimes provocative caricatures, which targeted prominent Russian political figures ranging from Putin and Soviet leader Joseph Stalin.

entries.title: New report raises concerns over Russian propaganda spread by Europe’s flagship AI company Mistral
entries.description: All four Mistral models evaluated by the Institute of the Estonian Language were deemed likely to use Russian propaganda in their answers.

entries.title: Swedish court imprisons man convicted of pressuring his wife to sell sexual services
entries.description: Prosecutor Ida Annerstedt told the AFP news agency at the start of the trial in April that the woman had "a serious fear" of her husband.

entries.title: Qatar 'cautiously optimistic' on US-Iran framework deal before Friday's signing
entries.description: Qatar said the framework agreement to be signed on Friday was a first step rather than a final settlement, adding that it would take time to return to "business as usual" even once the Strait of Hormuz reopens. Trump separately praised Qatar's mediating role and its "great bravery".

entries.title: Iran coach calls team World Cup's 'most oppressed' after rushed departure from US
entries.description: Amir Ghalenoei said his side was ordered to fly back to Tijuana hours after Monday's 2-2 draw with New Zealand, while captain Mehdi Taremi called it "a disaster for us," adding that the team's "goal and wish is to pursue peace and happiness".

entries.title: Lukashenka apologises to Zelenskyy, calls him 'young and inexperienced'
entries.description: Belarus leader Aliaksandr Lukashenka said that Belarus poses no threat to Ukraine as he apologised to Volodymyr Zelenskyy for his harsh comments in the past, while saying Ukraine’s president was “not a military man”.

entries.title: Lídia Jorge honoured with Austrian State Prize for European Literature
entries.description: It is a prize “awarded since 1965 for the literary work of a European author whose writing has gained international renown”, reads a note on Austria’s culture ministry website.

entries.title: Norway's Crown Prince Haakon cuts workload to be with gravely ill wife, palace says
entries.description: Mette-Marit, 52, was diagnosed in 2018 with a rare form of pulmonary fibrosis, or scarring of the lungs, that causes breathing difficulties.

entries.title: UK 'investigating' after yacht says Russian warship fired 'warning shots' in Channel
entries.description: The incident comes two days after armed British forces boarded and detained a sanctioned tanker in the Channel that is suspected of being part of Russia’s shadow fleet.

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | June 16th, 2026 – Evening
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 16th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: EU Parliament rejects member states’ first draft of long-term budget
entries.description: EU lawmakers called the document “insufficient” and criticised proposed spending cuts of €32.8 billion. Because the budget requires Parliament’s approval, pressure is mounting to reach an agreement by the end of 2026.

entries.title: Trump ballroom cost soars to €516m with half coming from taxpayers, US media say
entries.description: The project began last year when Trump, with little warning and without consulting Congress, tore down the entire historic East Wing of the White House.

entries.title: UK lions predict England's World Cup fate
entries.description: Lionesses and lions at a UK sanctuary were given World Cup-themed boxes to choose from, with keepers treating their picks as playful predictions for England's group-stage matches.

entries.title: Church of Sweden launches inquiry into PM's wife over unspecified ethical concerns
entries.description: The Lutheran Church of Sweden, which has some 5.4 million members in a country of 10.6 million, formally separated from the Swedish state in 2000.

entries.title: US support could be Ukraine's 'trump card' against Russia, says Ukrainian former Prime Minister
entries.description: Former Ukrainian Prime Minister Arseniy Yatsenyuk told Euronews that Moscow had expected a “spat” between G7 leaders and Volodymyr Zelenskyy, but said the “optics of the summit are much better than expected.” He added that the key question now is whether Ukraine and the EU can win Donald Trump’s sup

entries.title: At least 58 states and territories contaminated by landmines, UN human rights chief says
entries.description: At least 945 people were killed and 4,325 injured by landmines and explosive remnants of war in 2024 alone, the UN said, citing the Landmine and Cluster Munition Monitor.

entries.title: Banned but still paid: How disinformation accounts keep monetising on Facebook
entries.description: A new study shows that Facebook pages flagged more than 10 times for fake content by fact-checkers still make money off their content, including after suspension.

entries.title: UN chief visits Haiti as gang violence soars and number of displaced hits 1.5 million
entries.description: Guterres’ first stop was the headquarters of the new gang-suppression force, which the UN Security Council approved in September.

entries.title: FBI foils plot targeting White House UFC event, five arrested across multiple states
entries.description: Five suspects from Ohio, Missouri and California are in custody after the FBI learned of the threat four days before the South Lawn event.

entries.title: Lionel Messi ties Miroslav Klose for most World Cup goals as Argentina cruise past Algeria 3-0
entries.description: France finds a way to break a goalless first half as they cruise past Senegal, the African champions whose recent AFCON title was stripped on a technicality and awarded to Morocco, delivering a 3-1 win campaign opener.

entries.title: Kazakhstan’s skies welcome Central Asia’s first Paragliding World Cup
entries.description: Around 150 pilots from 33 countries have gathered in Kazakhstan's Zhetysu region for Central Asia's first-ever Paragliding World Cup.

entries.title: Germany and Poland set to boost military cooperation and shared duties in new defence deal
entries.description: Berlin and Warsaw are set to expand their defence collaborations in a new deal aiming to boost plans to protect the Baltic Sea and enhance military cooperation and communications between the two European allies.

entries.title: Patriot, IRIS-T, Flamingo: Germany's long-range weapons gap
entries.description: Global shortages of Patriot missiles are putting air defence systems under pressure. Germany's IRIS-T offers one answer, but Berlin still lacks the long-range firepower needed for deep strikes.

entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 17th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: Direct trains from Bucharest to Istanbul, Sofia and Varna have restarted until October
entries.description: Romania's national railway has relaunched daily direct summer trains from Bucharest to destinations in Türkiye and Bulgaria, with fares starting from €27.

entries.title: From Jeff Bezos to Europe’s AI push: Everything to know about VivaTech 2026
entries.description: Euronews will be at the tech conference, which is now in its 10th edition.

entries.title: Hands, feet and nose: Javier Bardem makes his mark in Hollywood and honours his family's legacy
entries.description: Javier Bardem was centre stage at a moving ceremony in Los Angeles as he paid tribute to his mother Pilar Bardem on Hollywood's Walk of Fame. But the Spanish actor departed from custom by pressing his nose into the wet cement along with his hand and footprints,

entries.title: How is the EU tackling drug trafficking and synthetic substance use?
entries.description: Europe's new drugs strategy runs to 2030, but critics warn it prioritises policing over public health. Watch the video!

entries.title: AI takes centre stage at G7 as Western fears over US ‘kill switch’ get real
entries.description: Western allies arrived at the G7 AI summit unsettled after the Trump administration cut off foreign access to Anthropic's most powerful models, confirming Europe's kill-switch fears and providing yet another boost for its tech sovereignty agenda.

entries.title: G7 summit: Leaders turn to economy as China Shock 2.0 looms
entries.description: The third and final day of the G7 summit in Évian, France, is underway. This morning's discussion is dedicated to the economic imbalances, as the China Shock 2.0 looms large over the largest Western economies.

entries.title: ‘Coolcations’ in Europe: New index reveals the best places to escape the summer heat
entries.description: A new summer heat escape index has ranked Europe's coolest destinations for summer travel in 2026, with Iceland, Finland and Norway leading the way.

entries.title: G7 leaders press Trump on Ukraine as summit wraps up
entries.description: The eyes of the world are on Evian-les-Bains, where G7 leaders are gathering for their third day and final day of talks. Beyond the photo ops, European leaders used this precious face time with the US President to urge him to break the deadlock on Ukraine.

entries.title: Iranian oil tankers pass US blockade ahead of peace deal signing ceremony
entries.description: The naval blockade began on 13 April and targeted all vessels entering or departing Iranian ports and coastal areas.

entries.title: Washington's Lincoln Memorial pool turns green days after renovation
entries.description: An algae bloom has turned Washington's newly renovated Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool green just days after a $14 million makeover.

entries.title: One killed in Russian drone strike on Ukraine's Zaporizhzhia
entries.description: Ukraine's Air Force said Russia attacked Ukraine with 119 drones overnight, of which 97 were intercepted. 20 drones were reported hitting 11 locations across Ukraine.

entries.title: G7 leaders agree to increase weapons deliveries for Ukraine, step up pressure on Russian economy
entries.description: G7 leaders pledge more weapons for Ukraine, including air defence and long-range systems, and signal readiness to tighten sanctions on Russia's oil and gas sector.

entries.title: Newsletter: G7 aligns on Ukraine in rare moment of unity
entries.description: Also in this newsletter: What to expect as G7 leaders meet top AI and tech executives.

entries.title: Germany reportedly prepping to take part in Strait of Hormuz safeguarding mission
entries.description: The German defence ministry has said naval units are already on standby for a possible deployment.

entries.title: Ukraine: Russian drone attack on Zaporizhzhia home kills one, injures seven
entries.description: At least one person was killed and seven others injured after a Russian drone struck a residential building in the southeastern Ukrainian city of Zaporizhzhia on 16 June, sparking a major fire and causing extensive damage, Ukrainian officials said.

entries.title: Zapatero appears before National Court as first former PM charged in Spain's democracy
entries.description: The former president will testify on Wednesday and Thursday over the Plus Ultra bailout and the jewels found in his Ferraz office, valued at more than one million euros.

entries.title: Watch: Cash, power, and politics—inside Gianni Infantino’s World Cup empire
entries.description: A massive revolt is brewing for football’s most powerful man. For many, it would be a career game changer. But will it be for Infantino?

entries.title: Will Cristiano Ronaldo finally win the World Cup?
entries.description: Ronaldo, now 41, is gearing up for what's set to be his final World Cup appearance. And it could be Portugal's best ever shot at lifting the trophy.

entries.title: Javier Bardem joins Hollywood greats at landmark ceremony
entries.description: Spanish film star Javier Bardem was honoured in Los Angeles on 16 June with a place in the celebrated forecourt of the TCL Chinese Theatre, a distinction reserved for some of cinema's biggest names.

entries.title: All of Uzbekistan readies to watch history at its first World Cup
entries.description: Schools, offices and city squares across Uzbekistan are setting up screens for Thursday's 7 am kick-off against Colombia, as the country makes its World Cup debut under coach Fabio Cannavaro. "In the heart of every Uzbek, this is a matter of pride and honour," an education ministry official said.

entries.title: Europeans head to World Cup in droves, but which country has seen the best supporter turnout?
entries.description: As the group stage heats up across Mexico, Canada and the US, Europe in Motion takes a look at the most committed supporters at the World Cup.

entries.title: France and Germany call for European AI sovereignty at VivaTech
entries.description: When the US suspended access to Anthropic's latest AI models last week, it reminded Europe that rules can change overnight. At VivaTech in Paris, France and Germany said the answer is sovereignty — and the window to build it is now.

entries.title: Putin meets with ASEAN leaders in Russia as G7 vow 'unwavering' support for Ukraine
entries.description: It comes after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy met with G7 leaders in France. Zelenskyy said on Wednesday they had agreed on the "additional strengthening of Ukraine’s air defense" and new measures against Moscow.

entries.title: New fleet of electric hydrofoil boats to be deployed in the Maldives
entries.description: The Maldives will introduce up to 100 electric hydrofoil boats, promising smoother and more sustainable transfers between its islands.

entries.title: Thousands flock to Art Basel in Switzerland as market outlook improves
entries.description: Art Basel 2025 in Basel draws thousands as high-end works by Picasso, Warhol and Hockney fuel a recovering global art market after years of decline.

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | June 17th, 2026 – Midday
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 17th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: ‘We have always been friends’: Trump and Meloni make amends at G7
entries.description: Trump and Meloni made amends at the G7 in Evian, joking about their recent spat over the Iran war. "We have always been friends," Meloni said.

entries.title: More people get news from social media than traditional outlets, study shows
entries.description: Fifty-four percent of people said they used social media as a news source at least once a week compared to the 51% using traditional media, like TV, radio or news websites.

entries.title: Hundreds of cats rescued from being eaten in Vietnam
entries.description: More than 500 cats were seized in Vietnam after police dismantled a cat meat trafficking network, with dozens of animals later reunited with their owners.

entries.title: Portugal urges EU rethink on carbon market cuts for industry
entries.description: Portugal has urged Brussels to pause cuts to free carbon allowances, warning they could damage competitiveness and slow industrial decarbonisation.

entries.title: Messi hat-trick fuels Argentina's World Cup title hopes
entries.description: Fans in Buenos Aires celebrated as Lionel Messi scored a hat-trick to lead defending champions Argentina to a 3-0 victory over Algeria in their World Cup opener.

entries.title: Four Seasons has just opened a luxury wellness resort on the Red Sea
entries.description: Guests can book in for a wellness journey that includes sunrise yoga on the beach, sauna and cold plunge sessions and healthy plates throughout the day.

entries.title: EU Parliament approves 'strictest-ever' migration law
entries.description: The European Parliament gave the final green light to a new EU law allowing controversial return hubs outside the bloc and home searches, as conservatives voted with far-right groups to pass the legislation.

entries.title: Jeff Bezos at VivaTech: We need to colonise the Moon to save Earth
entries.description: The Amazon founder told VivaTech in Paris that moving heavy industry off Earth is the only way to reconcile economic growth with a liveable planet — and the moon is where it starts.

entries.title: 'An unexpected story of friendship': Sean Penn to direct police officer drama on January 6 riots
entries.description: Sean Penn has signed a deal with Warner Bros. to direct a film he's scripted about the deadly attack on the US Capitol on January 6 in 2021.

entries.title: England out to avenge 2018 semifinal loss to Croatia as they start World Cup campaign
entries.description: England coach Thomas Tuchel said he expected former Real Madrid legend Luka Modrić and Manchester City's Mateo Kovačić to still be the "heartbeat" of the Croatian side.

entries.title: Glue, glorious glue: Sticking together the Sagrada Familia’s central towers
entries.description: Did you know that Loctite technology bonds were crucial in sticking stone and steel together in the Sagrada Familia temple’s 826 panels? At 172 metres tall, it is the world’s highest temple and was last week blessed by Pope Leo XVI.

entries.title: An influencer, a PR specialist and a bishop: Who are the latest EU-sanctioned disinformation actors?
entries.description: The Council of the European Union added a further 10 individuals and one entity to its sanctions list on 15 June, accusing them of conducting hybrid manipulation and foreign interference activities on behalf of Russia.

entries.title: This hotel chain just launched an ‘anti-appy hour’ to get guests off their phones
entries.description: Guests at YOTEL hotels will be able to block their access to social media apps on their phone using a Bloom card.

entries.title: Iran pledges no nuclear weapons as US commits $300bn, leaked deal draft shows
entries.description: The full US-Iran framework deal to end the war, as circulated during the G7 summit, would see Iran abandon its pursuit of a nuclear weapon in exchange for financial incentives to comply with the agreement.

entries.title: Trump jokes ‘I’m the boss’ as he arrives for G7 meeting
entries.description: Donald Trump joked that he was “the boss” as he arrived for a G7 meeting in France on Wednesday. The light-hearted remark came before leaders gathered for talks on trade, economic challenges and global financial stability alongside representatives from partner nations.

entries.title: Israeli settlers torch mosque in West Bank village of Jiljilya
entries.description: Residents of Jiljilya, north of Ramallah, awoke on 17 June to find their mosque set alight overnight, with burnt tyres, Hebrew graffiti reading "revenge" and extensive damage left behind in an area of the occupied West Bank where Israelis are prohibited from entering.

entries.title: Groundbreaking US astronaut Christina Koch wins top Spanish award
entries.description: Koch was part of the four-member crew of the Artemis II mission which in April flew around the Moon and is widely viewed as a potential candidate for future lunar-surface missions.

entries.title: Netherlands hosts Japan’s emperor Naruhito to boost 400-year ties
entries.description: The Netherlands welcomed Japanese Emperor Naruhito and Empress Masako to Amsterdam on 17 June during an official visit aimed at celebrating the long-standing relationship between the two countries.

entries.title: Afghan government staff told to abandon smartphones or face prison sentence
entries.description: How widely the rule is being implemented across Afghanistan and the scale of its impact were not entirely clear.

entries.title: Norwegian Crown Princess undergoes successful lung transplant
entries.description: The 52-year-old will remain in hospital for a number of weeks so that doctors can monitor for any complications, Arnt Fiane, a professor of medicine at the University of Oslo, said.

entries.title: Paris just opened a floating bookshop under Notre-Dame - and it has DJ sets
entries.description: The floating venue, called Nanna, offers more than 5,000 books in a self-service setup where visitors can borrow freely - with no obligation to return them.

entries.title: Albania's PM Edi Rama: Is Europe ready for its next Helmut Kohl moment?
entries.description: In Berlin, once the symbol of a divided Germany and Europe, Albania’s Prime Minister Edi Rama delivered a speech on Europe's future. Rama called on Europe to seize a “Helmut Kohl moment”. Euronews reports on the speech, delivered at the German Committee on Eastern European Economic Relations.

entries.title: Read the full text of the leaked 14-point US-Iran draft agreement
entries.description: It remains unclear whether the 14-point draft that was circulated at G7 meetings in France will be the version formalised at a ceremony in Switzerland on Friday.

entries.title: From regime change to regime deal? How Tehran's political camps are reacting to leaked US-Iran deal
entries.description: Iran's hardliners have framed the framework deal as a victory, even as Trump dismissed reports of a $300bn reconstruction fund and warned he would resume strikes if Tehran did not comply.

entries.title: UK minister ‘very confident’ of getting triple EU reset deal done at July summit
entries.description: Nick Thomas-Symonds tells Euronews he’s “very confident” London and Brussels will close new pacts on agri-food exports, electricity and youth mobility when they convene for a summit in July, as domestic pressure mounts on his government.

entries.title: Rutte heads to Washington to smooth NATO tensions ahead of July summit
entries.description: EXCLUSIVE: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte is travelling to Washington next Tuesday ahead of the annual NATO summit in Ankara in July. Relations between the US and its NATO allies have been strained after Trump accused European nations of abandoning the US during its war in Iran.

entries.title: Bus with Belarus children's football team hit by a drone in Russia, Moscow claims
entries.description: A bus carrying a children's football team from Belarus in Russia's border Bryansk region was hit by a drone, according to Russian officials. Moscow claims it was attacked by Ukraine, but Kyiv denies it carried out any operations in the area.

entries.title: Israel continues strikes on Lebanon ahead of anticipated US-Iran peace deal
entries.description: While violence has declined in Lebanon since a US-Iran agreement to end the Middle East war was announced on Monday, Israeli strikes on the south have killed at least five people since the deal, according to Lebanese state media.

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | June 17th, 2026 – Evening
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 17th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: Luigi Mangione to use psychiatric defence in health CEO murder trial, judge says
entries.description: Brian Thompson was killed in December 2024 as he walked to a Manhattan hotel for UnitedHealth Group’s annual investor conference.

entries.title: Germany and Poland sign new defence deal as balance of power in Europe shifts
entries.description: Poland’s importance as a logistics hub for Ukraine, alongside its growing economy and heavy defence investment, has made it a compelling partner for Germany and other core European countries.

entries.title: Zelenskyy heads to Brussels buoyed by new G7 commitments
entries.description: Ukraine secured fresh G7 military and diplomatic backing, including air defence support, as pressure grows on Russia to negotiate.

entries.title: How can Europe compete with the US and China economically? MEPs debate on The Ring
entries.description: From Artificial Intelligence to rare minerals, from Trump's tariffs to China's cheap exports - in this edition MEPs Jörgen Walborn (EPP) and Lina Gálvez (S&D) talk about the global economic challenges that lie ahead and that impact all of us in Europe.

entries.title: Six takeaways from the G7 summit in Évian
entries.description: G7 leaders gathered in France for a high-stakes meeting over the last three days to discuss Ukraine, the Middle East and world challenges. Here is what you need to know.

entries.title: Europeans leave G7 feeling relieved, balancing self-interest and flattery to keep Trump in check
entries.description: Macron hailed the G7 as an "objectively successful" summit after leaders agreed on key issues from Ukraine and Iran to critical minerals. European officials point to growing convergence with Washington, but Trump's volatile diplomacy continues to cloud transatlantic relations.

entries.title: Finland's parliament passes bill in support of lifting total ban on nuclear weapons
entries.description: Finland dropped decades of military non-alliance to join NATO in April 2023 in response to Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

entries.title: WFP welcomes €695m donation from US as it warns acute hunger to worsen in 13 hotspots
entries.description: The WFP has said it wants to reach 110 million people in the most acute need around the world this year, for which it would require €11 billion.

entries.title: Row between Elon Musk and German broadcaster ZDF sparks major controversy
entries.description: German broadcaster ZDF has reacted to a cease-and-desist suit from Elon Musk, deleting a line from a programme intro over his links to UK right-wing activist Tommy Robinson.

entries.title: AI dominates conversations at VivaTech
entries.description: VivaTech's 10th edition draws 200,000 visitors to Paris, with Jeff Bezos and Emmanuel Macron among the guests. AI dominates conversations on the show floor. Euronews finds a personal survival capsule for when the robots take over.

entries.title: Trump welcomed to Versailles for dinner with Macron
entries.description: Donald Trump was welcomed to the Palace of Versailles on Wednesday evening for a private reception and dinner hosted by French President Emmanuel Macron following the conclusion of the G7 summit in Evian.

entries.title: Trump signs initial deal to end Iran war and open Strait of Hormuz as nuclear talks continue
entries.description: Trump signed an initial framework deal to restore the pre-war status quo between Iran and the United States, inking an initial agreement towards ending a war he started with Israel on 28 February.

entries.title: Moment Trump signs US-Iran deal at Palace of Versailles
entries.description: Video released by the French presidency shows Donald Trump signing an agreement aimed at ending the war with Iran during a dinner at Versailles, with French President Emmanuel Macron seated beside him.

entries.title: Ronaldo fails to score in disappointing Portugal opener as England dismantle Croatia in 4-2 win
entries.description: Ronaldo, who matches Messi in a record sixth World Cup appearance, failed to become the first player to score in six different tournaments in his nation’s opening fixture against DR Congo.

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | June 18th, 2026 – Morning
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 18th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: European lawsuits over child suicide and self-harm caused by social media pile up
entries.description: Cases are being heard before courts in Italy and France, while the family of a Scottish teen is part of a lawsuit in the United States.

entries.title: These luxury European hotels have been included in a new hall of fame list
entries.description: From Paris and Monte Carlo to Lake Como and St Moritz, nine of Europe's most iconic luxury hotels have earned places in Esquire's Hotel Hall of Fame.

entries.title: Hidden figures: Stephan Gladieu on photographing the people of North Korea
entries.description: Ahead of his exhibition at the Musée des Confluences in Lyon, French photographer Stephan Gladieu talked to European Lens about his series titled ‘North Korea’, a collection of photographs taken over three years and several  trips to the reclusive country.

entries.title: EU leaders meet in Brussels to discuss Ukraine, China shock and €2 trillion budget
entries.description: Russia's war on Ukraine, unfair Chinese competition and the next long-term budget top the agenda of the EU summit.

entries.title: Synthetic opioids: can Europe keep up with a new drug created every week?
entries.description: Fifty new drugs were detected across Europe in 2025 alone, many of them highly potent synthetic opioids. Can the EU’s drugs strategy keep pace with an increasingly dangerous and fast-moving drug landscape?

entries.title: The world’s best skylines have just been named – and none of the top 10 are in Europe
entries.description: Asia dominated the list, with eight spots making the top 10 across China, Malaysia and Japan.

entries.title: Europe Today: NATO, Ukraine and the battle over Europe's future budget
entries.description: NATO defence ministers are gathering to prepare for next month’s leaders’ summit in Ankara. Among those attending is Pentagon chief and US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.

entries.title: Ukraine strikes Moscow oil refinery for second time this week
entries.description: It comes as Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy met with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte in Brussels.

entries.title: Newsletter: Zelenskyy in town for talks with NATO and EU leaders
entries.description: Also in this newsletter: Former US commander general Ben Hodges reacts to the signing of the US-Iran peace deal, and former French Economy Minister Bruno Le Maire tells Euronews the EU needs to function as a core group of six.

entries.title: Watch: Behind closed doors—Ukraine, taxes, and trade at this week's European Council Summit
entries.description: Tonight, Council President António Costa is gathering all twenty-seven national leaders for an exclusive, high-stakes working dinner. Sadly, he forgot to invite your reporter, but that doesn't stop us from seeing what will be served on the multi-course menu.

entries.title: UK: Tommy Robinson’s Oxford Union debate sparks protests and free speech backlash
entries.description: Tommy Robinson’s appearance at the Oxford Union prompts large protests and reignites the UK debate over free expression and platforms for extremists.

entries.title: Crimea will turn into an island, Ukraine’s defence minister says in a message to Russia
entries.description: Ukraine is methodically cutting off ground lines of communication for Russian forces into occupied Crimea, isolating the peninsula from Moscow’s forces and any supplies from Russia.

entries.title: Discover Lisbon like a local with our guide: Seven insider tips for its seven hills
entries.description: Venture off the beaten track and out of your comfort zone, dodge the crowds and, above all, steer clear of tourist traps. Here are seven ideas, one for each of Lisbon’s hills, devised by locals.

entries.title: Watch: Moment Trump signs US-Iran agreement at Versailles
entries.description: US President Donald Trump signed a memorandum of understanding with Iran at the Palace of Versailles on 18 June, marking a significant diplomatic step aimed at easing the Middle East conflict that has unsettled the region since February.

entries.title: Waldorf Astoria to open in London's iconic Admiralty Arch in autumn 2026 - in pictures
entries.description: Located on The Mall, the restored Grade I-listed landmark has been transformed into a luxury hotel with 114 rooms and suites, a spa, and two signature restaurants.

entries.title: 'The Ring' and 'Lilo & Stitch' actress Daveigh Chase dies aged 35
entries.description: Horror movie fans will recognize Chase from her performance as Samara Morgan, the main antagonist in American remake of 'The Ring'.

entries.title: Hegseth says Europe must take the lead on building 'NATO 3.0'
entries.description: US president Donald Trump has repeatedly pushed for European NATO allies to divert more money to defence spending.

entries.title: Mass protest by ultra-Orthodox Jews in Israel over army conscription
entries.description: Thousands of ultra-Orthodox Jews protest near Netanya against mandatory army service, demanding release of draft refusers amid Israel’s wartime strains.

entries.title: VivaTech 2026: Tips from L'Oréal and PwC on personal vitality and business resilience
entries.description: From skincare giant L'Oréal to consulting firm PwC, business leaders at VivaTech 2026 had a shared message: personal vitality and organisational resilience are no longer a luxury — they are a competitive necessity.

entries.title: Where is EV charging cheapest and fastest?
entries.description: Europe's EV charging network is growing fast, but prices and refuelling point density vary widely across the continent. Europe in Motion breaks down the data.

entries.title: Shipping industry wary of resuming Strait of Hormuz navigation despite imminent deal
entries.description: The world's most important energy chokepoint may soon reopen, but insurance markets must regain confidence for the shipping sector to resume normal navigation. Meanwhile, Germany has dispatched two naval vessels for a possible mine clearance operation.

entries.title: Video: Hundreds of drones target Russia in major Ukrainian attack
entries.description: Russian officials said Ukraine launched one of its largest drone attacks of the war. Images and video released by the Russian media showed massive fires raging at the Moscow Oil Refinery, located about 15 kilometers from the Kremlin. Thick black clouds of smoke rose over the city.

entries.title: Cristiano Ronaldo faces media backlash as pundits slam Portugal's display against DR Congo
entries.description: The 41-year-old Portugal captain was hit by a barrage of criticism after a display that fell short of expectations at what is his sixth and, possibly, last World Cup.

entries.title: Misinformation targets Trump and European leaders at G7
entries.description: Social media users have been sharing old clips and taking statements by politicians out of context, in an apparent effort to highlight the strained relationship between the US and Europe.

entries.title: Voters head to the polls in key UK by-election that could determine Britain's next PM
entries.description: Burnham is regarded by many Labour MPs as the party's best chance of recovery following the disastrous May local elections.

entries.title: Inside the ultra-luxe Swiss mountainside retreat where Iran and the US are set to meet
entries.description: Initial negotiations between the US and Iran on the final agreement are set to take place at the Bürgenstock Resort Lake Lucerne this Friday.

entries.title: Besides Trump, nobody believes deal will last, former US general says
entries.description: Retired US General Ben Hodges believes the situation is now "much worse" than four months ago. With Israel and Lebanon left out of the deal between the US and Iran, Hodges thinks the potential for Israel and Hezbollah to "resume fighting is really high".

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | June 18th, 2026 – Midday
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 18th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: Israel says one soldier killed and several injured in clashes following latest strikes in Lebanon
entries.description: Israeli officials close to Netanyahu say the Likud leader has no intention to give up positions in Lebanon and are engaged in negotiations with the US following the announcement of an initial deal with Iran to halt hostilities.

entries.title: ‘If Ukraine burns, so will Moscow,’ Zelenskyy says after refinery fire
entries.description: The Ukrainian president said on Thursday that Kyiv will respond to all Russian attacks and that the morning strike on Moscow’s refinery — in response to Russia's attack on a UNESCO-protected cathedral earlier this week was “entirely justified.”

entries.title: World's longest non-stop flight set for 2027 take-off as Qantas confirms Sydney-London route
entries.description: Qantas’s long-awaited ‘Project Sunrise’ will see the world's longest non-stop passenger flight cover more than 16,000 kilometres between 19 and 22 hours.

entries.title: US and Iran sign deal, but who really won? Here's what to know
entries.description: Trump signed the framework deal at Versailles while Pezeshkian signed in Tehran, pledging to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and triggering 60 days of further talks. Analysts say the terms leave Tehran in a stronger position than the deal's framing suggests.

entries.title: Kaja Kallas responds after Israeli foreign minister says he will 'sever all contact' with her
entries.description: Kallas said she valued "dialogue and engagement" with Israel, but she made no mention of the reported apartheid comment.

entries.title: England’s captain fantastic: Harry Kane praised for a ‘complete performance’ against Croatia
entries.description: The Bayern Munich striker scored twice as the Three Lions beat Croatia 4-2 in their World Cup group L opening fixture.

entries.title: Joel and Ethan Coen named as this year’s Lumière Award recipients
entries.description: The 18th Lumière Awards will honour not one but two giants of the silver screen...

entries.title: EU-backed green bonds risk financing Chinese clean tech in third countries
entries.description: The EU has launched a €15-20bn green investment fund to boost sustainable projects, but a good share of the money is set to go towards buying Chinese clean technologies – including the same high-risk power inverters Brussels is trying to phase out.

entries.title: From Foxconn to Nvidia: Why France is so attractive for Europe’s AI infrastructure
entries.description: Foxconn, Nvidia and Mistral AI announce major AI infrastructure deals at Europe's VivaTech conference, with France's cheap nuclear energy and homegrown talent drawing global investment.

entries.title: Salesforce France CEO: Both leaders and employees need to adapt to AI
entries.description: Speaking at Vivatech in Paris, Emilie Sidiqian, Salesforce France’s CEO, told Euronews Next how companies should embrace AI and why leaders must drive its adoption from the top.

entries.title: Archaeologists discover 5,000-year-old 'proto-Stonehenge' just a few miles from the famous monument
entries.description: The structure consisted of two wooden posts that aligned precisely with the summer sunrise and winter sunset, suggesting ancient Britons were already tracking the Sun before the creation of Stonehenge.

entries.title: Brussels rally presses EU to curb trade with settlements
entries.description: Hundreds of demonstrators gathered in Brussels on 17 June ahead of a European Union summit. Protesters call for sanctions on Israel and a ban on goods produced in Israeli settlements.

entries.title: Toronto celebrates as Ghana beat Panama with late World Cup goal
entries.description: Canada became the backdrop for emotional celebrations as Ghana secured a late World Cup victory over Panama in Toronto. Supporters from both nations turned the city into a vibrant meeting point before the decisive Group L match.

entries.title: Poland arrests suspect in killing of Russian artist critical of Putin, PM Donald Tusk says
entries.description: Since it launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022, Russia has been accused of trying to assassinate its opponents abroad, including targeting exiled activists in France and Lithuania.

entries.title: Euronews Culture's Film of the Week: 'Toy Story 5' - You've got a friend in tech?
entries.description: The toys face a new existential crisis: technology. As timely as it is, can the fifth instalment recapture the magic lacking in 'Toy Story 4'?

entries.title: Albania's PM Edi Rama in Berlin: Europe today needs courage for enlargement
entries.description: Is Europe ready for its next Helmut Kohl moment? Albania's PM Edi Rama makes his case in Berlin

entries.title: Moscow uses 'Russian Houses' in Africa to lure recruits into war in Ukraine, investigation shows
entries.description: Ukrainian military intelligence has revealed that Russia's expanding network of "Russian Houses" functions as a recruitment pipeline for its war, luring young Africans with promises of education and jobs before some are sent to the front lines or into drone factories.

entries.title: Graft probe into Spanish ex-PM Zapatero expanded to daughters and secretary, court says
entries.description: The former premier defended his innocence during a three-hour hearing before Calama on Wednesday, becoming the first former or serving Spanish head of government to declare as a suspect in a corruption investigation.

entries.title: Scotland for the win: Massachusetts becomes first US state to 'legalise' haggis
entries.description: The Scottish staple has been illegal in the US since the 1970s. Following a pre-World Cup campaign by one of Scotland's leading butchers and the efforts of a Scottish podcaster, Massachusetts has become the first US state to finally legalise haggis. Officially. Ish.

entries.title: Rutte tries to downplay Pete Hegseth's blistering attack on NATO
entries.description: As the Trump admininstration plans to pull a number troops out of Europe in the next six months, Secretary of War Pete Hegseth lambasted NATO defence ministers in Brussels, describing them as "shameful" and preoccupied with gender equality and climate change.

entries.title: Middle East flights update: Which airlines are returning to the region and when?
entries.description: While the US and Iran have signed a Memorandum of Understanding, European airlines are unlikely to restart flights until after the summer.

entries.title: Bulgarian prime minister opposes EU sanctions on Russia's Patriarch Kirill
entries.description: "The era of the Crusades is over," the Bulgarian prime minister said on Thursday as he confirmed his opposition to the proposal.

entries.title: Palestine Football Association denies reported FIFA youth match against Israel
entries.description: The PFA said it had "not received any official communication or proposal from FIFA regarding such a match".

entries.title: Chinese-British nationals jailed after being convicted of spying on exiled Hong Kong dissidents
entries.description: The sweeping National Security Law, which severely curtailed freedoms in Hong Kong, contributed to years of strained ties between Beijing and London.

entries.title: EU summit: Leaders and Zelenskyy to debate accession and peace negotiations
entries.description: Russia's war on Ukraine and unfair Chinese competition are expected to dominate the first day of the EU summit in Brussels.

entries.title: How do Iranians and Israelis view the US-Iran deal?
entries.description: Residents in Tehran and Tel Aviv voiced mixed reactions to the US-Iran agreement, with some welcoming an end to the fighting while others questioned whether the deal would hold.

entries.title: New Lithuanian coalition formed as Social Democrats strike deal with partners
entries.description: Lithuania’s Social Democrats have formed a third coalition in just one and a half years, clearing the way for a new cabinet to be led by party leader Mindaugas Sinkevičius.

entries.title: Germany and Uzbekistan deepen ties, President Steinmeier tells Euronews
entries.description: Trade between Germany and Uzbekistan exceeded $1.4 billion (€1.2bn) last year, while German investment reached $2 billion (€1.7bn). The two countries are also expanding educational exchanges, labour mobility programmes and business partnerships.

entries.title: The energy transition's next test is security
entries.description: 'Countries that emerge strongest from the next phase of the transition will be those with diversified energy systems that remain secure, affordable and sustainable in a more uncertain world' writes Roberto Bocca from World Economic Forum’s Centre for Energy and Materials in an OpEd for Euronews.

entries.title: Keeping up appearances: Greece reveals Parthenon façade after 220 years

The comments came after Iran and the US kickstarted a 6-day negotiating window to permanently end the war.

entries.title: Mandarin Oriental is set to return to this southeast Asian capital this winter

entries.title: How does the EU ensure pay transparency? Ask the Euronews AI chatbot
entries.description: The EU is rewriting compensation rules. Member states had until 7 June to implement the Pay Transparency Directive, which addresses pay transparency and gender pay gap. But what does it change in practice? Ask the Euronews AI chatbot.

entries.title: Europe Today: Starmer set to step down as US-Iran talks and Moldova summit unfold

entries.title: Spain pound Saudi Arabia 4-0 as Egypt tops Group G in matchday two fixtures of the 2026 World Cup

entries.title: Newsletter: A Commissioner's secret trip to Israel

entries.title: Can the EU fix the gender pay gap? Take our poll.

entries.title: Norway fans take over Times Square with Viking row

entries.title: The party is over: Why France is looking to tighten its grip on free parties

entries.title: Moldova's EU accession and Transnistria settlement are 'parallel processes', minister says

Moldova is working "tirelessly" to ensure all Moldovans benefit from accession, said the minister.

entries.title: 10 years of Brexit: Which campaign claims have come true?

entries.title: Octola III: Embrace Arctic ultra-luxury at this invitation-only retreat in Finnish Lapland
entries.description: Opening in December, this private lodge accommodates just six guests and access is granted exclusively through an invitation-only waiting list.

entries.title: Sesame Street scandal: Elmo’s World Cup support reveals Republican ‘hypocrisy’

entries.title: Inside Ukraine’s campaign to turn Russian-occupied Crimea into an 'island'
