entries.title: UN launches Hormuz evacuation for 11,000 stranded seafarers
entries.description: The operation follows a peace agreement between the United States and Iran that ended months of conflict and allowed commercial shipping to resume through one of the world's most important maritime routes.

entries.title: Von der Leyen to visit Pashinyan in Yerevan to build on Armenia’s pro-EU vote
entries.description: As the EU rolls out a support package for Armenia, European Commission President von der Leyen is set to meet re-elected Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan in Yerevan after he secured a decisive pro-West mandate from the Armenian people, sources confirmed to Euronews.

entries.title: Japan's emperor and empress arrive in Belgium for state visit
entries.description: The arrival of Japan's emperor and empress in Belgium marks a symbolic moment for two countries whose partnership stretches back more than a century and a half.

entries.title: Iceland kills first whales since 2023, resuming whaling
entries.description: Iceland's Marine and Freshwater Research Institute has recommended that no more than 150 fin whales are caught in the 2026 season.

entries.title: UNAIDS warns Trump's HIV funding cuts to South Africa could cost lives
entries.description: The Trump administration says the phased withdrawal is linked to policy disputes with Pretoria, which rejects the allegations and says it is pursuing greater self-reliance.

entries.title: Belgian royals roll out red carpet for Japan's emperor
entries.description: Japanese Emperor Naruhito and Empress Masako received a ceremonial welcome at the Royal Palace of Brussels as they began a state visit to Belgium.

entries.title: Giant Messi statue honors Argentina's World Cup hero
entries.description: A giant mural near Buenos Aires and a towering statue in Patagonia highlight Lionel Messi's enduring status as a symbol of unity and pride for Argentines during the World Cup.

entries.title: Cristiano Ronaldo scores at his sixth World Cup
entries.description: Cristiano Ronaldo scored as Portugal beat Uzbekistan in their World Cup group match in Houston, becoming the first footballer to score at six World Cup tournaments.

entries.title: Airbus under fresh scrutiny as EASA orders A380 inspections over wing cracks
entries.description: The aviation giant is already battling supply chain problems and growing costs across its operations.

entries.title: Technical malfunction with communications sytem temporarily halts entire German railway network
entries.description: Deutsche Bahn said its services were resuming “step by step” after an almost three hour outage which froze all of its operations nationwide.

entries.title: US eases restrictions on Iranian national football team allowing travel two days before next match
entries.description: The US Department of Homeland Security says it had always intended on easing restrictions on the Iranian team after ensuring that safety protocols were up to order.

entries.title: New giraffe born in Denmark zoo
entries.description: At Copenhagen Zoo, a giraffe calf is born as staff and visitors witness the moment. The mother immediately cares for her newborn.

entries.title: From misting fans to wearable air conditioners: Here’s the tech to keep you cool this summer
entries.description: Euronews Next takes a look at some of the tech to keep you cool this summer.

entries.title: Europe's best hotel swimming pools: Take a dip and stay cool in the heatwave
entries.description: Europe boasts some of the world’s most stunning hotel swimming pools. From Italy to Iceland, we take a look at the perfect spots to keep cool during the summer months.

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | June 24th, 2026 – Morning
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 24th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: Romeo Beckham to make acting debut in queer tennis film ‘Forty Love’
entries.description: After pursuing careers in football, tennis and modelling, 23-year-old Romeo Beckham is making his acting debut in a new French film, set to be released later this year.

entries.title: Europe Today: Sánchez under pressure as Europe backs Ukraine
entries.description: Europe's five leading military powers meet in Berlin to discuss support for Ukraine and tougher sanctions on Russia, while US-Iran negotiations continue in Switzerland. Meanwhile, Spain's Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez faces pressure over a widening corruption scandal.

entries.title: Rock in Rio 2026: Live music still draws thousands
entries.description: The opening weekend of Rock in Rio drew thousands of visitors from 125 countries, at a time when music and live shows fit into a mobile phone.

entries.title: Newsletter: Ukraine tensions and the Taliban question
entries.description: In today's newsletter: All eyes are on Berlin to see if Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk can resolve tensions between the capitals, and further questions loom regarding a meeting between the European Commission and the Taliban on Tuesday.

entries.title: Ronaldo shines as Portugal deliver 5-0 thrashing of Uzbekistan and Engand suffer 0-0 draw to Ghana
entries.description: Ronaldo was quick to shutdown criticism over his goalless World Cup opener and overall usefulness to his national side as he grabbed a brace in Portugal’s 5-0 pounding of Uzbekistan.

entries.title: 'The Blair Witch Project’ movie reboot will haunt cinemas in 2027
entries.description: The reimagining of the horror feature that became a worldwide sensation in 1999 is set to be released next year. Can it succeed where previous sequels failed?

entries.title: Toronto streets fill with Panama and Croatia supporters
entries.description: Supporters of Panama and Croatia turned downtown Toronto into a sea of flags and chants ahead of their crucial World Cup Group L clash.

entries.title: Europeans want less dependence on foreign technologies, study finds
entries.description: Europe relies heavily on foreign tech, but citizens increasingly want EU-controlled alternatives. How willing are Europeans to pay the price for digital sovereignty?

entries.title: Mosul celebrates Iraq's World Cup return
entries.description: Hundreds of fans gather in Mosul to watch Iraq face France at the World Cup, waving flags, singing and turning a city square into a lively fan zone.

entries.title: IAEA chief says nuclear inspections of Iran's enrichment sites 'going to happen' despite Tehran push
entries.description: Rafael Mariano Grossi cited the signed US-Iran memorandum of understanding, pushing back against Tehran's claim that bombed enrichment sites are off-limits to inspectors.

entries.title: IAEA chief says inspections of Iran's nuclear sites 'going to happen'
entries.description: IAEA's Grossi pushed back against Tehran's claim that no agreement has been reached to allow inspectors to inspect bombed enrichment sites, citing the signed US-Iran framework deal.

entries.title: ‘Reality check’ for England: Is it time to unleash Saka and Rashford?
entries.description: Head coach Thomas Tuchel urges England fans to remain positive after the Three Lions were held to a frustrating goalless draw by Ghana in World Cup group L.

entries.title: Iran stronger after conflict, warns former Iran negotiator Wendy Sherman
entries.description: In an interview with Euronews’ flagship morning show Europe Today, former US diplomat Wendy Sherman welcomes the current talks aimed at ending the US-Iran conflict between but warns that Tehran appears to be in a stronger position.

entries.title: Thailand’s ‘Michael Jackson’ jailed over fatal drunk-driving crash
entries.description: A criminal court in Bangkok has sentenced Manasawin Nantasen, known as Tik Shiro, to two years in prison for "drunk driving resulting in loss of life".

entries.title: Dead tree painted red to highlight climate change impact
entries.description: A painted red oak at Kew Gardens, killed in the 2022 heatwave, stands beside a resilient “Tree of Hope”, highlighting climate change impacts in the UK.

entries.title: Péter Magyar quotes Donald Tusk: 'The V4 is back'
entries.description: At the Grassalkovich Palace in Gödöllő, the Hungarian prime minister opened the post‑Visegrád Four talks press conference with the Polish PM’s Brussels remark.

entries.title: Anthropic's Mythos AI found flaws in classified US systems within hours, officials say
entries.description: Mere hours, not weeks — that is how long it took an Anthropic AI model to find vulnerabilities across classified US government systems.

entries.title: Pedro Sánchez tells Congress: 'I never knew of, nor would I have tolerated, any of these practices'
entries.description: Spain's prime minister faced opposition calls to resign as he defended his government over a string of corruption cases involving his party, partner and brother.

entries.title: Rutte meets Trump to defuse tensions ahead of major NATO summit
entries.description: The Secretary General's meeting with Trump comes ahead of a potentially bruising leaders' summit in July. Relations between the Trump administration and Europe remain fractious over the war in Iran, with the US cutting its European defence commitments drastically.

entries.title: Seal at Hungarian zoo brushes teeth
entries.description: A seal at Nyíregyháza Zoo in Hungary brushes its teeth in the Arctic Ice World exhibit, showcasing daily care and animal enrichment activities.

entries.title: Paris neighborhood fighting the heatwave with with 'Ciné-clim' initiative
entries.description: In order to combat the days’ highest temperature peaks, one cultural initiative in Paris has everyone asking: “Why isn’t this nation-wide?”

entries.title: Kazakhstan signs €10bn in deals with Europe as Tokayev wraps Brussels visit
entries.description: During President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev's visit to Brussels, strategic documents were signed with the EU, and 30 commercial deals were concluded with EU companies, with the EU being the Central Asian country's largest trading and investment partner.

entries.title: Cate Blanchett launches free tool to help people protect identity from AI
entries.description: The acclaimed Australian actor presented her Human Consent Registry at the European Parliament. The tool allows people to protect their likeness from being used by AII.

entries.title: Twelve EU countries seek green funds beyond 2030 to cope with energy transition
entries.description: The member states are historically more dependent on fossil fuels than other EU countries, and have benefited since 2021 from special funding to cope with the accelerating shift to renewable energy sources.

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | June 24th, 2026 – Midday
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 24th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: World's fastest supercomputer is now from China, surpassing US and Germany
entries.description: One of the most interesting use cases for the supercomputer is that it is key to developing models used in artificial intelligence (AI).

entries.title: Heatwave and wildfire alerts, power cuts in Finistère: France swelters
entries.description: Temperatures of 40 to 45C are forecast in France on Wednesday 24 June, wildfire alerts are in force in the centre and 68,000 homes in Finistère are without power.

entries.title: Gulf banks want AI, but can they keep control of customer data?
entries.description: As banks in the Gulf explore new AI tools, the focus is increasingly shifting from what the technology can do to how it can be used responsibly.

entries.title: UAE trains connecting Abu Dhabi and Dubai set to launch this September
entries.description: Etihad Rail will start its route between the UAE capital and Fujairah in late June, with further stations opening in the coming months.

entries.title: Trump vs algae: Viral reactions as fencing goes up around Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool
entries.description: The Trump administration has been losing a war with algae in the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. Critics think it’s the perfect metaphor for Trump’s tenure as president, and the latest move to cover up the embarrassment has sent the internet into meltdown.

entries.title: Ten years after Brexit, travel rules confusion remains widespread
entries.description: From passport validity rules and healthcare cover to new EU border checks and visa requirements, many British travellers are still unsure what Brexit means for their European holidays.

entries.title: Hollywood stars oppose AI: how a new register protects artists' identities
entries.description: A new free tool allows artists to decide whether or not to give the green light for their image to be used by artificial intelligence.

entries.title: Stars including Cate Blanchett and Javier Bardem unite to protect their identities in AI era
entries.description: A new free tool allows artists to give or withhold consent for their image to be used by artificial intelligence.

entries.title: Consenting adults: Cate Blanchett launches project to allow artists to choose AI identities
entries.description: Hollywood heavyweight Cate Blanchett is behind a new free online tool which allows artists to give or withhold permission for their image to be used by artificial intelligence.

entries.title: Giesinger celebrates Munich beer culture, pushes for Oktoberfest place
entries.description: Munich Brewers’ Day 2026 brings processions, ceremony and beer celebrations to Marienplatz and Viktualienmarkt, honouring the city’s brewing guild tradition.

entries.title: Greek Supreme Court rejects Laura Kövesi appeal over EU prosecutors' terms
entries.description: Greece's top court ruled the EPPO lacked legal standing to challenge a domestic decision to limit prosecutors' terms to two years instead of five.

entries.title: Meloni-Trump spat: What do the polls say about Meloni's popularity?
entries.description: A dispute between Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and US President Donald Trump has sparked debate about her standing with voters.

entries.title: The ‘Aperol Spritz index’: European cities to visit for an affordable aperitivo
entries.description: If you love the bright orange beverage but don’t love the price tag attached to it in many bars, here’s where you should be heading.

entries.title: Zuckerberg wants Meta to launch its own prediction market, report says
entries.description: Prediction platforms Polymarket and Kalshi have made record profits this year, and now Mark Zuckerberg has reportedly directed tech giant Meta to build their own.

entries.title: National Portrait Gallery removes artwork after row over Churchill's legacy
entries.description: The London-based museum has faced controversy over a video installation blaming Winston Churchill for a colonial-era famine in India.

entries.title: Didier Deschamps to miss France's match against Norway following the death of his mother
entries.description: The France head coach has returned to France to attend his mother’s funeral. He is set to return to Boston on Saturday, the day after the French team's game against Norway.

entries.title: Evo Morales warns of civil war from Chapare stronghold: 'I will not surrender'
entries.description: Evo Morales speaks out from hiding in the Chapare jungle as Bolivia enters its seventh week of fuel, food and medicine shortages.

entries.title: Senior UK minister rules out leadership challenge as Burnham edges closer to Downing Street
entries.description: Jones' decision removes a major obstacle standing between Burnham and the UK's top job and could mean the latter is in Downing Street as early as July.

entries.title: EU scales back Ukraine's accession timetable due to Hungarian resistance
entries.description: Faced with renewed resistance from Hungary, the EU has been forced to change plans and now aims to unblock two, rather than five, accession clusters with Ukraine and Moldova before the summer break.

entries.title: Tehran labels framework deal 'US defeat' as Trump threatens to halt talks
entries.description: Iranian key negotiator hailed the US–Iran framework deal as a “declaration of US defeat,” while Trump warned that talks would end immediately if reports that Tehran is seeking to impose tolls on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz prove to be true.

entries.title: EU diplomatic service announces new top posts with strong defence focus
entries.description: The EEAS has named Kajsa Ollongren as secretary general, with Matti Maasikas and David Cvach in deputy roles. The defence-focused appointments come as the bloc's foreign policy machinery faces increasing pressure.

entries.title: European heatwave forces Eiffel Tower early closure and tourist attraction shutdowns
entries.description: From the Eiffel Tower and the Louvre to Buckingham Palace's Changing of the Guard, soaring temperatures are forcing attractions to close, shorten opening hours and cancel events as a heatwave grips Europe.

entries.title: Madrid, Woody Allen's new cinematic muse
entries.description: Woody Allen’s next film, set entirely in Madrid, will treat the city as a star. Filming begins in October, following in the footsteps of New York, Paris, Rome and Barcelona.

entries.title: Two workers for a Norwegian demining charity killed in Russian strike on Ukraine
entries.description: The NPA has more than 450 staff based in Ukraine, where it has been carrying out mine clearance activities since 2023.

entries.title: First whales killed as Iceland restarts commercial hunt
entries.description: Iceland resumes commercial whaling after a two-year pause, with two fin whales processed at the Hvalur facility, reigniting controversy and protests.

entries.title: Paris's Cirque d'Hiver begins renovation to restore its original splendour
entries.description: Twenty ornamental frescoes from the Second Empire are to be restored over twelve to eighteen months. The seating, the polychrome ceiling canvas and the stained-glass windows will also be refurbished.

entries.title: Portugal's World Cup victory sparks celebrations in Lisbon
entries.description: Thousands of fans in Lisbon celebrated Portugal's 5-0 World Cup victory over Uzbekistan, with Cristiano Ronaldo scoring twice and becoming the first player to score in six different World Cup tournaments.

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | June 24th, 2026 – Evening
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 24th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: London's Met police to scale up use of drones and live facial recognition
entries.description: UK civil liberties campaign group Big Brother Watch has criticised the use of technologies such as drones and facial recognition.

entries.title: IMF to closely monitor China’s economic growth amid trade tensions
entries.description: An accurate measurement of China's digital economy is a priority for other powers competing with Beijing in fast-developing technologies.

entries.title: Special Report: Brexit, ten years on: could the UK ever rejoin the EU?
entries.description: Today's Special Report is anchored by Mared Gwyn and features correspondents Maria Tadeo and Shona Murray, alongside top interview guests, for a deep dive into one of the most consequential political decisions in modern European history: Brexit and the United Kingdom's departure from the EU.

entries.title: German authorities carry out raids over alleged Russian gas sabotage
entries.description: An investigation is looking into the sale of Gazprom's Berlin-based subsidiary Gazprom Germania, which took place around a month after Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine in February 2022.

entries.title: Europe's five largest military powers meet ahead of key NATO summit with Ukraine in mind
entries.description: The leaders of Europe's largest military powers met in Berlin on Wednesday in preparation for a pivotal NATO summit next month, to discuss support and security guarantees for Ukraine and how to strengthen the Atlantic Alliance's European Pillar.

entries.title: UK records hottest June day ever as Europe swelters in heatwave
entries.description: The Met Office has warned that "exceptionally warm" temperatures are expected to continue through the middle of the week.

entries.title: Has Brexit made Europe stronger or weaker? MEPs go face-to-face on The Ring
entries.description: What has Brexit taught Europe about sovereignty, migration, economic competitiveness, democratic accountability, and disinformation? Has it strengthened the case for deeper European integration? MEPs debate the future of EU-UK relations on this edition of The Ring.

entries.title: Scientists find human DNA over 2,000 years old in caves in Spain and Portugal
entries.description: A research team based in Cáceres has found human DNA more than 2,000 years old on cave walls in Spain and Portugal, a discovery that could transform the study of prehistory.

entries.title: Catholics walk barefoot in mud ritual
entries.description: Devotees in rice paddies cover themselves in mud and banana leaves for the Taong Putik festival, honouring St. John the Baptist with barefoot processions.

entries.title: Hormuz confusion deepens as Iran and US clash over who controls it
entries.description: Trump said Tehran had promised no tolls or charges in the strait, hours after Iran and Oman announced a joint permit system asserting their authority over the waterway. Rubio flew to Abu Dhabi to reassure Gulf allies, as analysts warned control over Hormuz would be a major strategic victory for Iran

entries.title: At least 32 killed and 700 injured in powerful Venezuela earthquakes
entries.description: Residents of the capital Caracas were forced to evacuate their residences and seek shelter outdoors as the quakes, which struck less than 200 km from the capital, caused widespread damage.

entries.title: No Zelenskyy in Gdansk: can Ukraine’s recovery forum ease Warsaw–Kyiv tensions?
entries.description: The Ukraine Recovery Conference opens in Gdansk on Thursday amid an unprecedented spat between Kyiv and Warsaw. With both presidents absent, attention will focus not only on reconstruction initiatives but also on efforts to ease increasingly strained bilateral relations.

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | June 25th, 2026 – Morning
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 25th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: France's largest startup campus Station F turns nine. Here's why it's nervous about the election
entries.description: The biggest threat to France's startup scene isn't the market or AI. It's the ballot box, according to data from startup incubator Station F.

entries.title: Washing underwear in kettles and raiding the breakfast buffet: Strange hotel guest habits revealed
entries.description: Using kettles to wash underwear, smuggling food from the breakfast buffet and squeezing extra guests into rooms are among the hotel habits Britons have admitted to in a new study, which comes alongside a new etiquette guide from Hotels.com and manners expert William Hanson.

entries.title: Global Beatles Day: Who was the walrus?
entries.description: On the inaugural Global Beatles Day, we unpack one of the most enduring mysteries surrounding the legendary band...

entries.title: Mumbai monsoon floods streets and disrupts travel
entries.description: Heavy monsoon rains flood Mumbai, submerging roads, disrupting traffic and forcing residents to wade through waterlogged streets in India’s financial hub.

entries.title: Which EU countries fight pay gaps? New transparency rules split the bloc
entries.description: Only four EU countries have implemented the EU’s Pay Transparency Directive, missing the June deadline meant to boost enforcement of equal pay rules across the bloc. Watch the video.

entries.title: Europe Today: Ukraine seeks defence, energy deals in Poland as Macron meets Meloni in France
entries.description: The Ukraine Recovery Conference 2026 kicks off today in Gdańsk, with Kyiv aiming to secure deals on defence capabilities, energy and economic cooperation. Meanwhile, France’s President Emmanuel Macron welcomes Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni to the French Riviera today for a major summit.

entries.title: Mexico, Switzerland and Brazil top their groups after final matchday of World Cup group stages
entries.description: Brazilian superstar Neymar Jr made an emotional World Cup debut after earlier doubts saw him on the cusp of missing out due to a recent string of injuries and unconvincing performances.

entries.title: Popular Ibiza DJ named as alleged 'mastermind' behind international drug trafficking operation
entries.description: Popular Ibiza DJ Hugo Bianco has been named in a major Spain-US drug trafficking case. He is the alleged “mastermind” of an international drug trafficking network, and faces a nine-and-a-half prison sentence.

entries.title: Newsletter: Can the Recovery Conference mend a fraying partnership?
entries.description: In today's newsletter: A high-level conference in Gdańsk has been plagued by no-shows from the Ukrainian government, NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte unveils "Trump Trillion" charts while at the Oval Office, and a Commissioner heads to Malta to address corruption.

entries.title: Trump says 'courageous' Zelenskyy is 'doing pretty well' in war with Russia
entries.description: "You have to say he's courageous, he's got great equipment, he's got great men, he's got fighters," Trump said of Zelenskyy.

entries.title: Moment earthquake hits Venezuela, sending people fleeing in panic
entries.description: Images showing cracked walls, collapsed ceiling panels and debris strewn across parts of Simón Bolívar International Airport highlighted the scale of damage after two powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela on 24 June 2026.

entries.title: Heatwave scorches Europe from London to Madrid as temperatures near 39C
entries.description: A powerful heatwave swept across Europe, stretching from France to Spain and Belgium and pushing temperatures towards 39C, raising concerns about public safety and pressure on infrastructure.

entries.title: Higher penalties, more time to prosecute: Child sexual abuse victims welcome new EU directive
entries.description: New rules greenlighted this week will cover the use of AI in child abuse cases and considerably extend the limitation period to a minimum of 32 years.

entries.title: AmaRudi: Europe's largest river cruise ship to launch on the Danube next spring
entries.description: The AmaRudi, AmaWaterways’ second double-width vessel, will accommodate 196 guests across 98 staterooms and suites when it launches in April 2027.

entries.title: North Korean soldier suspected of defecting crosses demilitarised zone into South Korea
entries.description: According to data from South Korea's Ministry of Unification, more than 34,000 North Koreans have defected to the South since its records began in 1998.

entries.title: Pay transparency isn't only about women. It exposes inequalities affecting millions of workers
entries.description: The EU's pay transparency rules can help close the gender pay gap and expose how Europe systematically underpays young, migrant, disabled and racialised workers.

entries.title: Fact check: Viral drone interception video shows Russia's Yolka system
entries.description: A video widely shared on social media appears to show a handheld device intercepting a drone. While some users have hailed the system as a Ukrainian military innovation, others have claimed it is Russian technology. The Cube traced the footage to determine the origin and purpose of the device.

entries.title: Pro-Israel Spanish journalist Pilar Rahola accused of complicity in genocide
entries.description: Barcelona’s public prosecutor has opened proceedings against Rahola over allegations of incitement to hatred and complicity in genocide.

entries.title: Ukraine gets €3.2 billion from EU support loan, von der Leyen says
entries.description: The announcement marks the first disbursement under the €90 billion loan. A second payment worth about €6 billion and dedicated to drone production is expected to be unveiled in the coming days.

entries.title: Eleven EU countries call for methane rules pause amid energy security fears
entries.description: The eleven EU countries say that the methane rules could jeopardise natural gas supply, especially from foreign suppliers who would face extra costs.  Environmentalists warn the EU not to cave in to pressure while the United Nations urged world nations to do more to mitigate methane emissions.

entries.title: Two years after Gen Z uprising, Kenyan protesters return to Nairobi streets
entries.description: Two years after Kenya's 2024 Gen Z tax revolt shook the nation, protesters gathered in Nairobi on 25 June to seek justice for victims and press for greater government accountability.

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | June 25th, 2026 – Midday
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 25th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: Why are Boston bars running out of Scottish beer?
entries.description: Boston bars say Scottish fans have sparked a shortage of Tennents Lager during the World Cup, with some pubs running low as thousands of supporters descend on the city. One bar says it may be the last in Boston still serving the popular Scottish beer.

entries.title: Qatar fans hopeful about the future despite World Cup exit after Bosnia defeat
entries.description: Qatar's World Cup dream came to an end after three group matches, with fans in Doha reflecting on the team's strong performance while remaining optimistic about the future, as Qatar emerges from the war with hope.

entries.title: Macron says France intercepted a Russian shadow fleet oil tanker off coast of Sicily
entries.description: It comes after the UK announced earlier this month that it had intercepted and boarded the Russian shadow fleet tanker "Smyrtos" in the English Channel.

entries.title: US-Iran talks could derail fragile peace process, says ex-Israeli spokesperson
entries.description: Former Israeli government spokesperson Eylon Levy believes Israel's campaign is "creating the urgency and opportunity" for Lebanon to confront Hezbollah. Levy said that if Lebanon is "capable of dismantling Hezbollah", it could mark the beginning of "a beautiful peace between Israel and Lebanon.

entries.title: Phoebe Bridgers announces new album after solo hiatus
entries.description: The American singer is coming back with her third solo record 'Lost Weekend' ahead of a US and European tour starting in September.

entries.title: SpaceX has quietly launched a spacecraft almost no one knew existed
entries.description: Rumours have circulated that it could be used to transport military hardware.

entries.title: Mediterranean travel trends: How ‘blue mind experiences’ and local culture keep people coming back
entries.description: As fly-and-flop holidays fall out of fashion, travellers are looking for more meaningful travel experiences even while prioritising sun and sea.

entries.title: Europe summer travel trends: Short breaks, family holidays and coolcations drive demand
entries.description: From quick getaways to heatwave-avoiding escapes, new data from Trip.com reveals the trends that are shaping summer travel across Europe in 2026.

entries.title: Bigger than Jupiter, lighter than cotton candy: Astronomers discover two ‘super-puff’ planets
entries.description: The two planets orbiting a star 1,110 light-years away are the biggest exoplanets ever found with a density lower than cotton candy, and may be mostly made of hydrogen and helium.

entries.title: International community pledges aid to Venezuela after devastating earthquakes
entries.description: The United States Geological Survey has estimated that the final death toll could be between 10,000 and 100,000 people.

entries.title: How much do we really know about microplastics? | Euronews Tech Talks
entries.description: Microplastics pollute our planet, raising questions about their impact on human health and the environment. This Q&A episode of Euronews Tech Talks sums up what science knows, and does not know, about them.

entries.title: IRGC warns ships off Oman's corridor in Hormuz as Rubio draws line on tolls
entries.description: The IRGC warned that any vessel navigating outside Tehran's designated routes was acting illegally, directly rejecting Oman's newly announced temporary corridor, as Rubio told a GCC meeting the US would not accept fees regardless of how they are framed.

entries.title: Central Asia’s first Paragliding World Cup is taking place in Kazakhstan
entries.description: Kazakhstan’s Zhetysu region is hosting the first Paragliding World Cup in Central Asia, bringing together around 150 pilots from 33 countries. The main discipline of the World Cup is cross-country flying.

entries.title: Leonard Cohen estate slams use of 'Hallelujah' at Trump's Freedom 250 rally
entries.description: Donald Trump had announced earlier in June that 'Hallelujah' would be performed at the opening of the Great American State Fair, fuelling the long-term feud between the US president and the estate of Leonard Cohen.

entries.title: Moment French navy boards suspected Russian 'ghost fleet' tanker
entries.description: The French navy boarded a tanker suspected of belonging to Russia's so-called "ghost fleet" in the Mediterranean, the fifth such operation since September 2025, as Paris steps up efforts to enforce sanctions linked to the war in Ukraine.

entries.title: Laissons Parler Les Gens: Podcast addresses issues facing young Africans online
entries.description: Youssoupha acknowledged cyberbullying and harassment were part of the challenges facing young Africans today, but he urged the youth to focus on the positives.

entries.title: James Bond search enters final stage with planned August screen tests
entries.description: Production on Bond 26 is set to begin next year. The expectation is that the official announcement for the next 007 actor will happen by the end of 2026.

entries.title: Brussels tightens rules on US cloud providers as it pushes for tech sovereignty
entries.description: The European Commission has decided that Microsoft Azure and Amazon Web Services should face strict rules to improve cloud competition. The move risks angering Washington as Brussels pushes the European tech sovereignty agenda.

entries.title: La Guaira, Venezuela’s port city, shows extensive earthquake damage after the quake
entries.description: New images from La Guaira on 25 June revealed widespread destruction after twin earthquakes struck Venezuela's Caribbean coast, damaging buildings, roads and key public infrastructure.

entries.title: 'I'm in great shape,' boxing legend Floyd Mayweather tells Euronews ahead of Athens fight
entries.description: Floyd "Money" Mayweather is set to face Greek fighter Mike Zambidis in Athens on Saturday 27 June.

entries.title: NATO chief Rutte defends allies as Trump says he was 'let down' on Iran
entries.description: Rutte pushed back gently against Trump's latest NATO complaints, saying, “I know there have been isolated cases about which you are really disappointed, but generally speaking, your European allies have been there with you."

entries.title: Heat stress threatens 130 million European workers, trade union body warns
entries.description: The European Trade Union Institute (ETUI) has presented a proposal for European legislation to protect workers' health during heatwaves.

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | June 25th, 2026 – Evening
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 25th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: Cargo ship hit by projectile in Hormuz as Iran issues renewed passage warning
entries.description: The British military said a cargo ship on the new Omani UN-backed route sustained bridge damage from a projectile, hours after the IRGC warned that vessels without Iranian permission were transiting "illegally".

entries.title: Why travel has become one of the best ways to make new friends

entries.title: Europe Today: Trump's tariff threat, Iran talks and Europe's heatwave

Attention also turns to growing EU-US trade tensions and the debate over how Europe should adapt to extreme temperatures.

entries.title: Australia and Vanuatu sign deal barring foreign military base on Pacific island
entries.description: China formed policing ties with Vanuatu in 2023 and has donated equipment including drones, patrol boats and vehicles to its police force.

entries.title: Saunasaari: A sauna island just opened in the middle of Helsinki

entries.title: ‘Blazing Saddles’ named funniest film of all time by AFI as Mel Brooks turns 100
entries.description: Mel Brooks, who turned 100 on Sunday 28 June, has been honoured by the American Film Institute, which named his 1974 Western satire the "funniest film of all time" - knocking 'Some Like It Hot' off the top spot.

entries.title: Can the EU stop SHEIN, Temu, and Aliexpress from dominating the e-market?
entries.description: The EU is swamped with cheap parcels imported from Chinese e-commerce giants. Products from SHEIN, Temu and the like have been benefitting from a tax loophole that will soon be axed by the EU.

entries.description: The Manifesto Library is located inside Livraria Lello in Porto, and is dedicated to books “that challenge power, censorship, exclusion, and dominant narratives.”

The dramatic weather followed days of record-breaking heat in France, where authorities say the extreme temperatures contributed to a sharp rise in deaths.

entries.title: Heatwave: Berlin police use water cannon to cool Bruno Mars fans

entries.title: 'Michael' overtakes 'Oppenheimer' to become highest-grossing biopic of all time

entries.title: US marks 250th anniversary in Brussels with messages of transatlantic solidarity

entries.title: Kazakhstan and UAE launch construction of €1.2bn wind farm

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | June 29th, 2026 – Midday

entries.title: Venice Commission experts to investigate Magyar's push to remove Hungary's president
entries.description: The Venice Commission will visit Hungary on Thursday to assess a constitutional amendment that would remove President Tamás Sulyok from office. Both the president and PM Péter Magyar's government say they are open to talks with the delegation.

entries.title: No Iran-US escalation boosts hopes for diplomacy, analyst says

entries.title: Starmer resigns: Which European country has the highest turnover of leaders?

entries.title: First Antarctic dinosaur fossil confirmed nearly 40 years after discovery

entries.title: Fines doubled as teens outsmart Australia's world-first social media ban

entries.title: Morocco fans light up Monterrey before Netherlands knockout clash

entries.title: These are the world’s most expensive holiday destinations to visit in 2026

entries.title: Thai airline employee arrested in Australia on suspicion of smuggling €302,000 of heroin

entries.title: Aerial images reveal scale of destruction after Venezuela earthquakes

entries.title: Ukraine's battered energy grid braces for 'intense heat' as extreme temperatures head east

entries.title: At least five people killed in shooting in northern Germany, police say
entries.description: Police said they were working to establish the background to the shooting and what exactly happened.

entries.title: Australian charged with murder after Thai teen's body found in suitcase

entries.title: The Strawberry Moon rises tonight — and no, it isn't strawberry-coloured

entries.title: Burnham vows to 'rewire Britain' with devolution push and 'No. 10 North' in Manchester

entries.title: Cyprus’ tourism revenues fell by more than 35% in April as a result of the war

entries.title: Hot wax: Are your vinyls at risk during a heatwave?

Climate policy is no longer judged solely by its environmental ambitions, but also by whether it can reduce emissions without deepening social inequalities.

entries.title: Neither pro nor con: EU declines to take stand on AC debate amid brutal heatwave

entries.title: China understands food security — the EU does not

The EU is structurally dependent on imported high-protein feed to raise farmed animals, particularly soy and maize, writes Nico Muzi in an OpEd for Euronews.

Germany’s trade Minister Katherina Reiche also met Wentao, with her ministry calling on Beijing to ensure “a level playing field".

entries.title: Over 1 million migrants apply for Spain's mass regularisation, PM Pedro Sánchez says
entries.description: Sánchez has long argued that immigrants are needed to sustain the economy, the welfare state and pensions amid Spain's ageing population and depopulation in rural regions.

The visit follows four days of strikes in the Strait of Hormuz that paused on Monday.

For Brussels, it is time to act. Europe needs a smart strategy of cooperation that will keep the European economy alive.

entries.title: Thousands soaked in wine at Spain's iconic festival

entries.title: AI helps scientists decipher papyrus scroll burnt in Vesuvius eruption

entries.title: UK watchdog moves to break Apple and Google's grip on app payments

entries.title: Can humans outshine AI?: EY's Julie Linn Teigland knows the secret of success

entries.description: WhatsApp says the new feature will be released over the coming months and that it will notify users when it is available in their country.

entries.title: Prosecutors conduct Europe-wide raids over alleged misuse of funds by defunct far-right EU group
entries.description: Raids were underway in France, Spain, Italy and Belgium as part of a probe into the alleged misuse of European parliament funds by the former far-right Identity and Democracy (ID) group.

entries.title: France's Emmanuel Macron speaks out against global rise in executions
entries.description: "The death penalty has never made a society safer," Macron said during a speech at the ninth World Congress against the death penalty.

entries.title: Death toll from Venezuela earthquakes climbs to 1,943
entries.description: UNICEF has estimated that around 1.8 million people, including 680,000 children, require humanitarian assistance following the quakes.

entries.title: France sweep past Sweden as Belgium prepare for Senegal test
entries.description: Kylian Mbappé scored twice and Bradley Barcola added a third as France beat Sweden 3–0 to reach the World Cup last 16. Meanwhile, Belgium takes on Senegal tonight in a high-stakes knockout clash in Seattle.

entries.title: US lifts export controls on powerful AI models, Anthropic says
entries.description: Anthropic will restore global access to its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models after the US Department of Commerce lifted security restrictions that blocked the powerful systems earlier this month.

entries.title: Kazakhstan looks to boost oil exports via Baku-Tbilisi-Ceyhan pipeline after Georgia talks in Astana
entries.description: In high-level talks held in Astana, Kazakhstan and Georgia agreed to strengthen economic cooperation, including plans to increase oil shipments via the BTC pipeline. The two countries also discussed agricultural development, investment expansion, and cooperation in digital services.

entries.title: Five pressing tasks for Ireland as it takes over EU Council presidency
entries.description: Ireland has kicked off its six-month presidency of the EU Council. From accession to the budget, here are the five most pressing tasks.

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | July 1st, 2026 – Morning
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this July 1st, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: White Pass & Yukon Route: One of the world’s most scenic rail journeys returns for summer
entries.description: Built in 1898 during the Klondike Gold Rush, the iconic White Pass & Yukon Route offers journeys from Skagway in Alaska into British Columbia and Canada's Yukon Territory.

entries.title: Almost one in three Europeans expect standard of living to decline, survey says
entries.description: Rising prices and the cost of living top the concerns in almost all EU countries, according to the new Eurobarometer. In France and Austria, pessimism could be linked to a negative image of the European Union.

entries.title: Ukrainian volunteers in Poland support front despite growing war fatigue
entries.description: A December survey by CBOS, an independent Polish research centre, found that 48% of Poles support accepting Ukrainian refugees, the lowest level since the start of the war.

entries.title: Drone captures rivers of lava flowing down Mount Etna
entries.description: Mount Etna in Sicily erupts, sending bright orange lava down the volcano's slopes as drone footage and ground images capture the spectacular flow.

entries.title: International Joke Day: What is the funniest joke in the world (courtesy of Monty Python)?
entries.description: In 1969, the world was introduced to a joke so good, so funny, so ruthlessly powerful that anyone who read or heard it would die from laughter...

entries.title: EU slaps €3 duty fee on SHEIN, Temu and AliExpress imports
entries.description: A new €3 customs duty takes effect across the EU on 1 July, ending years of tax-free shopping from platforms like SHEIN, Temu and AliExpress and signalling the end for ultra-cheap online hauls. Watch the video.

entries.title: Europe Today: EPP boss Weber speaks exclusively to Euronews as von der Leyen visits Baku
entries.description: Manfred Weber, President of the European People's Party, speaks exclusively to Euronews as European Commission chief Ursula von der Leyen visits Azerbaijan to build on EU influence in a region traditionally in Russia's orbit.

entries.title: NoMad Hotels to debut in Singapore complete with craft soft-serve sundae-slash-cocktail bar
entries.description: The 173-key property on Orchard Road will open in late 2026, bringing with it three restaurant and bar concepts plus a poolside lounge.

entries.title: A 'prolonged' and 'dangerous' heatwave is set to intensify across the US
entries.description: The director-general of the WHO said the organisation has partnered with FIFA to help protect players, staff and fans at the 2026 World Cup from the scorching temperatures.

entries.title: Newsletter: EU entering trade ‘conflict’ with China without autumn deal, EPP boss Weber says
entries.description: Hello, it’s Mared Gwyn here with your morning digest. Today: The view from Dublin as Ireland takes over the EU presidency baton, and how EU allies have clinched better terms as the bloc’s steel tariffs kick in.

entries.title: Netflix faces backlash over AI-generated Gene Wilder voice for new Willy Wonka series
entries.description: The voice of deceased actor Gene Wilder has been recreated using AI for a new Willy Wonka-themed competition show on Netflix... Fans are not happy that the eccentric chocolatier’s voice has been recreated.

entries.title: ‘Immediately was enchanted’: Viral Puerto Rico song gets its own music video shot on island
entries.description: The song creator Bill Stiteler worked with Discover Puerto Rico to create a video that highlights some of the island’s top tourist attractions.

entries.title: Where do graduates struggle the most to find a job?
entries.description: Europe in Motion breaks down employment and unemployment data across Europe.

entries.title: 'We cannot kill our industry due to climate change,' Manfred Weber says
entries.description: European People's Party President Manfred Weber has told Euronews that EU climate policies must be "reasonable from a business perspective" and has criticised Spain's large-scale plan to regularise undocumented migrants.

entries.title: Mexico fans celebrate World Cup win over Ecuador
entries.description: Fans in San Diego erupt in celebration as Mexico beat Ecuador 2-0 in a World Cup knockout match, ending a 40-year drought in the competition.

entries.title: EU set for trade 'conflict' with China unless deal reached by autumn, EPP chief Weber tells Euronews
entries.description: In an exclusive interview with Europe Today, Manfred Weber warns the EU is heading into a "phase of conflict" with Beijing unless both sides strike a deal to significantly reduce trade imbalances by the autumn.

entries.title: Village People lead singer and 'Y.M.C.A.' co-writer Victor Willis dies aged 75
entries.description: Willis was a founding member of Village People, whose hits include ‘Y.M.C.A.’, ‘Go West’ and ‘In The Navy’.

entries.title: Three die in Mexico City World Cup street celebrations
entries.description: Emergency crews were called to the Juárez area of the capital after three people were reported to be unconscious.

entries.title: Manfred Weber attacks Pedro Sánchez on migration as EPP takes a tougher stance
entries.description: The EPP leader slammed Spain’s regularisation of undocumented migrants in an exclusive interview with Euronews. Weber's party is calling to suspend the right to asylum in specific circumstances.

entries.title: Russia closes railway border crossings with Finland, Estonia and Latvia
entries.description: In an unexpected move, Russia announced several railway border crossings with Finland, Estonia and Latvia to be “temporarily” shut down. Could this indicate an upcoming mobilisation in Russia as Moscow lacks troops for its ongoing all-out war against Ukraine?

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | July 1st, 2026 – Midday
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this July 1st, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: Pope calls on Lefebvrists to halt bishop ordinations
entries.description: The Pope has issued a direct and personal appeal, urging the traditionalists to return to full communion with the Catholic Church.

entries.title: Visa’s new platform promises travel perks in European destinations
entries.description: The new Visa Destinations platform brings together dining, shopping, entertainment, culture, hotel and wellness recommendations and offers for travellers. It’s initially rolling out across 10 major destinations, including three  in Europe.

entries.title: First David Hockney painting sold at auction since artist’s death avoids ‘posthumous premium’
entries.description: David Hockney’s "The Only One with Waves" (1991) was inspired by the wild Pacific Ocean – marking the moment he transitioned away from his iconic still swimming pools.

entries.title: EU seeks to deepen ties with Turkey amid geopolitical volatility
entries.description: Three EU commissioners met the Turkish president in Ankara to improve cooperation on trade, migration and security with a key candidate for EU accession ahead of next week's NATO summit.

entries.title: Transhumance tradition returns to French Alps
entries.description: Sheep and goats are guided into Alpine pastures at Le Collet d’Allevard as France’s traditional transhumance festival returns in the Belledonne massif.

entries.title: Did The Simpsons really predict the final of the 2026 World Cup?
entries.description: Some people believe that they know who will face off in the final of this year’s FIFA World Cup – all because of an episode of The Simpsons from 1997. The claim is going viral on social media, and many are affirming that this is the US animated sitcom predicting the future once more...

entries.title: Nine of the world’s top 10 strongest passports are European, new index reveals
entries.description: The Global Passport Index assesses visa-free access as well as investment attractiveness and quality of life.

entries.title: World Cup 2026: Is heat the tournament's 'hidden opponent'?
entries.description: As the World Cup 2026 advances to the next stages, high temperatures could hinder players’ performance. But not all teams are equally exposed, according to new analysis.

entries.title: EES at a 'critical point': Europe's aviation sector urges EU to show more flexibility
entries.description: In an open letter, Europe's airports, airlines and aviation bodies have urged the European Commission to temporarily suspend the Schengen Entry/Exit System (EES) during peak periods, warning that border delays of up to five hours are disrupting passengers, flights and tourism.

entries.title: Several people dead after blaze breaks out at Antwerp apartment block, police say
entries.description: Police warned locals affected by the smoke to close windows and doors and to shut off ventilation systems if necessary.

entries.title: Water cannons cool tourists at Rome's Colosseum amid heatwave
entries.description: Tourists at Rome's Colosseum cool off with water spray and bottled water as civil protection teams respond to temperatures approaching 40C.

entries.title: Ship runs aground in Hormuz as Iran insists on controlling the strait
entries.description: Iran's IRGC said the grounding was caused by the vessel straying from its approved corridor. Hormuz control and the Israeli intervention against Hezbollah in Lebanon remain the two main obstacles to a final deal.

entries.title: Two dead after storms batter Austria and Romania
entries.description: Bucharest was hit particularly hard by storms overnight, with flooding damaging buildings and vehicles across the capital.

entries.title: 'Bruising' film about OpenAI picked up by indie studio after Amazon snub
entries.description: Neon has bought Luca Guadagnino's unflinching drama about Sam Altman, weeks after Amazon walked away from the nearly finished film following its $50 billion tie-up with OpenAI.

entries.title: EPP pushes to soften EU carbon market reforms in bid to shield industry
entries.description: The largest political group in the European Parliament has urged the Commission to extend free carbon allowances for some industry sectors beyond 2030, according to a document seen by Euronews.

entries.title: Mourners attend funerals for 14 Pakistani children killed in tutoring centre roof collapse
entries.description: Police are investigating whether negligence during ongoing construction work caused the collapse that left another eight children hospitalised in stable condition.

entries.title: Ireland 'ambitious' to open more negotiation chapters with Ukraine during EU presidency
entries.description: Ireland's Minister of State for European Affairs and Defence, Thomas Byrne, said the country's six-month EU presidency will be a "generational moment" with key priorities, including opening more negotiation clusters with Ukraine and strengthening the EU's economy.

entries.title: Tourist takes Sorolla painting lost in Seville home to Murcia: 'I thought the frame was cool'
entries.description: A 57-year-old tourist from Murcia picked up in Seville a painting left on the street as its owners loaded their car. He took it home, unaware it was a valuable work being sought by police.

entries.title: Venice Romance: Andrea Bocelli celebrates 30th album anniversary with concert at Piazza San Marco
entries.description: From crossover classics to stunning special guests, the Italian opera star Andrea Bocelli has left Venice swooning after an amazing show in celebration of the 30th anniversary of his pioneering album Romanza

entries.title: Ukraine hits Russia's Ufa oil refinery for second time in a week, Zelenskyy says
entries.description: Ukraine’s domestically developed and manufactured drones and missiles have been hammering Russian oil facilities, including refineries, terminals, storage depots and pipeline pumping stations, for months.

entries.title: Mattel releases new Barbie in partnership with Miley Cyrus
entries.description: Mattel releases yet another celebrity Barbie - this time with the American singer-songwriter and actress Miley Cyrus.

entries.title: O Canada! Eurovision Song Contest welcomes first new country since 2015
entries.description: Canada will sing at next Eurovision Song Contest after the country's public broadcaster confirmed full membership of the European Broadcasting Union - allowing them to compete in the world's biggest competition of its kind.

entries.title: Just a few social media posts from self-styled 'experts' can strongly influence opinion, study says
entries.description: Researchers warn social media users can form strong opinions after viewing only a few social media posts on a topic. Across social media, many self-proclaimed experts and influencers are plugging a gap amid declining public trust in traditional experts.

entries.title: Finnish entrepreneur jailed for breach of EU sanctions after exporting trucks to Russia
entries.description: Finland's 1,340-kilometre border with Russia has been closed since December 2023 after the arrival of around 1,000 migrants without visas.

entries.title: Firebomb attacks in northern Greece target residences of governing party members
entries.description: Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis expressed "outrage and anger" at what he called a "cowardly, terrorist and murderous attack."

entries.title: 'Εβίβα!' It's official: Europe's Best Bar is in Greece
entries.description: The inaugural list of Europe’s 50 Best Bars has been revealed, and it was a great night for the Greek capital Athens...

entries.title: Ukraine’s former commander-in-chief to run for president, reports say
entries.description: Valerii Zaluzhnyi, former commander-in-chief of Ukraine's Armed Forces and now Kyiv’s envoy to the UK, is reportedly willing to run in the next presidential election once the situation allows. Zaluzhnyi is seen as Zelenskyy’s main rival.

entries.title: England breaks record for warmest June since records began, Met Office says
entries.description: Scientists warn that extreme weather events such as heatwaves are becoming more frequent and intense as a result of human-induced climate change.

entries.title: Russian gas imports rise despite EU phase-out
entries.description: Russian liquefied natural gas continues to arrive mainly through Spain, France, Belgium and the Netherlands, while pipeline deliveries remain concentrated in Hungary, Slovakia and Greece.

entries.title: 20 million children use AI and adopt it faster than adults, UNICEF says
entries.description: New UNICEF analysis finds children are adopting AI faster than adults, with millions turning to it for homework help and, in some cases, personal advice — as safeguards for young users lag badly behind.

entries.title: Bayeux Tapestry in London: queues of up to nine hours to book tickets
entries.description: The British Museum website, where the tapestry will be on display from September 2026 to July 2027, has attracted tens of thousands of visitors keen to buy tickets.

entries.title: Bayeux Tapestry in London: up to nine-hour wait to book tickets
entries.description: The British Museum website, where the tapestry will be on display from September 2026 to July 2027, has attracted tens of thousands of visitors keen to buy tickets.

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | July 1st, 2026 – Evening
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this July 1st, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: Trial begins for suspected mastermind behind killing of Maltese journalist Daphne Caruana Galizia
entries.description: Caruana Galizia had exposed corruption at the highest level in the country, shining a spotlight on murky links between Malta's business and political elites.

entries.title: European Parliament to start procedure to ban Alternative for Germany’s EU party
entries.description: Most of the political groups are expected to approve a process that could lead to stripping the far-right Europe of Sovereign Nations of its status as a European political party and consequent funding.

entries.title: Planned 1.7 million satellites 'devastating' for astronomy by making night sky brighter
entries.description: There are also concerns that huge amounts of space debris from satellites could increasingly crash into each other in a dangerous chain reaction known as "Kessler syndrome."

entries.title: Albania NATO summit hangs in balance as US slams security 'free riders'
entries.description: The proposed host country of next year's meeting hasn't shown credible progress on the alliance's new defence spending target of 5 percent of GDP.

entries.title: Man charged with sabotage of Nord Stream gas pipeline in German court
entries.description: Nord Stream is a network of pipelines running under the Baltic Sea from Russia to Germany to provide Western Europe with natural gas, meaning it provided billions to the Kremlin's coffers.

entries.title: MEPs clash in The Ring over the right answers to Europe's scorching heat dome
entries.description: A record-breaking heatwave is baking Europe - have politicians failed to protect citizens? Is air-conditioning the answer? In this edition, Benedetta Scuderi (Greens) and Andrea Wechsler (EPP) talk about the political consequences of a (failed?) climate policy.

entries.title: Ukraine launches 'transparent mechanism' allowing partner countries to buy weapons, Kyiv says
entries.description: According to the most recent reports, Ukraine is now producing over 4 million drones annually and could double that number with sufficient funding.

entries.title: 'England's superstar': Harry Kane the hero as Three Lions survive shock against DR Congo
entries.description: Thomas Tuchel’s England are through to the World Cup round of 16 after two late goals from captain Harry Kane saw the Three Lions come back to beat DR Congo 2-1.

entries.title: Nicolás Maduro sued in US court over alleged extrajudicial killings in Venezuela
entries.description: Maduro is currently in a New York jail awaiting trial on criminal charges of drug trafficking after the US military removed him from office in an extraordinary raid in Venezuela in January.

entries.title: California to institute Bruce Lee Day, first for a Chinese-American in state history
entries.description: In the 1960s, Lee found work in Hollywood, notably as Kato in the TV series "The Green Hornet," but studios wanted him to play racist stereotypes and paid him less than his white counterparts.

entries.title: Two arrested after unfurling 'power of love' banner on Empire State Building antenna
entries.description: Office workers wondered how the pair managed to get to the antenna of a high-profile building where visitors are screened and told not to bring large packages, sports equipment, costumes or masks, among other items.

entries.title: EU looks to South Caucasus for solutions as von der Leyen holds talks with President Aliyev
entries.description: Von der Leyen begins her South Caucasus tour in Baku as the EU steps up engagement on regional stability, energy and connectivity.

entries.title: Committee tasked with post-war Gaza governance says Cyprus talks 'highly productive'
entries.description: Participants reviewed plans for reconstruction, security and governance, while developing a framework to ensure transparency and accountability for international donors.

entries.title: Russian missile and drone strikes kill 13 in Kyiv after Zelensky warns of massive attack
entries.description: "During the night, the enemy once again launched a massive attack on the Kyiv region using strike drones, ballistic missiles and cruise missiles," regional governor Mykola Kalashnyk said on Telegram.

entries.title: US-Iran parallel talks in Qatar end with pledge to keep talking
entries.description: Holding separate meetings in Qatar, the US and Iran made progress and agreed that the next meeting would be after Khamenei's funeral, host Qatar confirmed.

entries.title: Venezuela death toll rises to 2,295 as medical crisis widens
entries.description: Doctors fear a severe medical crisis in Venezuela following the 24 June earthquakes that killed 2,295 and injured more than 11,000. Thousands of displaced survivors are living in crowded shelters without clean water.

entries.title: Is talking about 'slow tourism' elitist, or just the sector's latest trend?
entries.description: Travel platforms are rushing to praise slow travel as the must‑try trend for 2026, citing its potential environmental and psychological benefits. But can everyone afford it?

entries.title: US, Belgium and England all survive tough tests to reach World Cup Round of 16
entries.description: England and Belgium narrowly escaped shock Round of 32 eliminations on Wednesday. Harry Kane's late double rescued England in a 2–1 win over DR Congo, while Belgium overturned a 2–0 deficit to edge Senegal 3–2 in extra time.

entries.title: Travelling to Croatia? Here are the best places to visit beyond the tourism hotspots
entries.description: Looking to discover Croatia beyond the headline hotspots? Euronews Travel asked the country’s tourist board which under-the-radar destinations travellers should visit and the events worth planning a trip around.

entries.title: Russia's petrol crisis worsens as citizens feel the bite of Moscow's war in Ukraine
entries.description: A third of Russia's refining capacity has been knocked out by Ukrainian drone attacks, causing petrol shortages across more than half of Russia's regions.

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | July 2nd, 2026 – Morning
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this July 2nd, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: EXCLUSIVE: EU set to reallocate Western Balkans funding in favour of accession 'frontrunners'
entries.description: The EU will redirect Western Balkans reform funding towards frontrunners Montenegro, Albania and North Macedonia, as other countries are struggling to keep up with reform deadlines.

entries.title: Europe Today: Clash over EU cash set to dominate today's Budget Conference
entries.description: The battle over the EU's next long-term budget will be on full display at today's Annual EU Budget Conference as member states clash over spending priorities, Ireland takes over the Presidency of the Council of the European Union, and we speak to the EU's head of aid operations in Venezuela.

entries.title: All members of Venezuelan nu-metal band Van Der Dijs confirmed dead in devastating earthquakes
entries.description: The band Van Der Dijs were in their rehearsal space at the time of the natural disaster.

entries.title: AirTags in luggage actually helps, with a 90% reduction of truly lost bags in a year
entries.description: Using a location tracking device has reduced recovery times by 26%, according to the SITA 2026 Baggage IT Insights.

entries.title: Newsletter: EU enlargement climbs the political agenda
entries.description: Good morning, I’m Mared Gwyn. Today: An exclusive on Brussels' planned redistribution of Western Balkans funding, why enlargement dominated the opening of Ireland's EU Council presidency, and a preview of the EU budget conference kicking off in Brussels.

entries.title: Don't block Ukraine's path to EU accession, Zelenskyy asks Hungary and Poland
entries.description: Facing resistance from Hungary and pushback from Poland, Volodymyr Zelenskyy said accession should be based on "rules" rather than "emotions".

entries.title: Ancient Rome's 'Google Maps' is here and working: Omnesviae
entries.description: A new digital tool lets users explore the Roman Empire’s road network and, using historical data, estimate travel times between cities 2,000 years ago.

entries.title: Ancient Rome's version of Google Maps: how long to reach the beach
entries.description: A digital tool lets users explore the Roman Empire’s road network and, using historical data, estimate how long journeys between cities took 2,000 years ago.

entries.title: Ahead of the 250th anniversary of American independence, how do Europeans see Trump and the US?
entries.description: Views of the US are shifting across Europe, with confidence in Donald Trump and America's reliability eroding over time. How do individual European countries compare?

entries.title: Couple climbs Empire State Building, gets engaged, then arrested
entries.description: Two climbers scaled the Empire State Building's antenna, displayed a peace banner and appeared to get engaged before being arrested by New York police.

entries.title: Alleged member of hacking group 'Scattered Spider' arrested in Finland
entries.description: Scattered Spider, also known as “Octo Tempest,” “UNC3944,” or “0ktapus,” is a group of criminal cyber actors that attempts to extort ransoms from companies.

entries.title: Trump was paid over $10 million by Amazon for 'Melania' documentary
entries.description: Trump’s cryptocurrency earnings were disclosed this week, leading to scathing criticism online. Part of the financial disclosures reveal how much he was paid by Amazon for this year’s Melania documentary.

entries.title: How NASA, Microsoft and the EU use AI to speed up post-quake rescues in Venezuela
entries.description: Artificial intelligence is now a key ally for emergency crews. Space agencies, tech firms and international bodies use satellite images and AI to map the areas hardest hit by the quake.

entries.title: Fast fashion: 69% of our clothes contain toxic chemicals
entries.description: The European Union is implementing a new tax on imports, to limit purchases from fast-fashion e-platforms like SHEIN, Temu, and similar. Their bargain price tags hide a toxic reality, as testing reveals these ultra-cheap garments carry a hidden cost to human health.

entries.title: EU Budget Commissioner Piotr Serafin criticises ‘frugals’ over proposed long-term budget cuts
entries.description: The so-called "frugal" member states are asking a significant reduction in spending ahead of the EU's upcoming 2028-2034 budget.

entries.title: 'Major health crisis looming over Venezuela,' EU Civil Protection expert warns
entries.description: An EU expert who is on the ground in Venezuela to coordinate the bloc's response to the earthquake told Euronews that "the clock is ticking", adding that the nature of the support provided will shift to focus on medical assistance.

entries.title: Dream job alert: Get paid to travel around Europe talking to strangers
entries.description: Self-improvement app RiseGuide is hiring a ‘Global Charisma Scout’ who will create an index of where on the continent it’s easiest to strike up a conversation.

entries.title: US fans celebrate World Cup last-16 spot
entries.description: US supporters celebrate in Carson, California, after the United States beat Bosnia-Herzegovina 2-0 to reach the World Cup round of 16.

entries.title: 'Terrible judgement': Queen Camilla faces backlash after royal meeting with J.K. Rowling
entries.description: Queen Camilla recently spent time with J.K. Rowling to mark the start of Royal Week in Scotland. This has sparked criticism, leading many to call out the Royal Family for displaying poor judgement - especially at the end of Pride month.

entries.title: EU Court of Justice rejects Google’s appeal against record €4.1 billion fine
entries.description: Google CEO Sundar Pichai has argued that the company's Android system has created "more choice, not less".

entries.title: Kazakhstan starts living by new Constitution, sets date for parliamentary elections
entries.description: Kazakhstan's new Constitution entered into force on July 1, prompting the Kazakh President to set August 23 as the date for parliamentary elections required under the new constitutional framework.

entries.title: Smoke rises over Kyiv after Russian overnight strikes
entries.description: Smoke rises over Kyiv after a large-scale Russian missile and drone attack overnight that killed at least eight people and injured dozens.

entries.title: Bulgaria aims for 'no further cuts on cohesion' in EU's next budget, minister says
entries.description: Bulgaria's Deputy Prime Minister Atanas Pekanov said the EU must "retain policies like cohesion" in its next long-term budget. While acknowledging negotiations "will take months", he backed reaching a deal by the end of the year, saying it is "better to reach a solution fast".

entries.title: EU targets Russian drone-makers with sanctions following massive strikes on Kyiv
entries.description: The new sanctions will target Russia's military-industrial complex, including firms that manufacture Shahed and Geran drone components.

entries.title: Jurassic fashion? T-Rex leather could reshape luxury industry says VML's Dimitri Guerassimov
entries.description: To create the leather, scientists started with fragments of fossilised T-Rex collagen, then used AI and computational biology to reconstruct the missing genetic code.

entries.title: Latest news bulletin | July 2nd, 2026 – Midday
entries.description: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this July 2nd, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entries.title: Vatican excommunicates Lefebvrists over unauthorised ordinations
entries.description: It comes after the controversial ordination of four new bishops carried out without papal approval.

entries.title: Window to control AI is closing and it could widen inequality, UN experts warn
entries.description: UN warns AI could widen the gap between rich and poor countries in a new report.

entries.title: 'Are we serious about war?': EU defence chief calls on countries to live up to security ambitions
entries.description: The Defence Commissioner warned that member states cannot bang the drum for a stronger security architecture while cutting the EU budget, and urged spending that matches their new defence ambitions.

entries.title: How the Entry-Exit System is becoming a nightmare for Europe's summer travellers
entries.description: The digital system for checking non-EU travellers’ documents is creating bottlenecks and glitches just as the peak of the holiday season approaches. As queues grow longer and passengers get more frustrated, airlines are calling for the option to suspend the checks in July and August.

entries.title: Tavolara glamping: Government revokes special economic zone approval in Sardinia, no luxury resort
entries.description: A luxury resort financed by Brazilian investors was planned, with special economic zone status to bypass landscape and environmental rules. Civic and environmental groups forced the government to revoke the permits, and the regional authority has appealed to the administrative court.

entries.title: Fast-moving wildfire spreads across southern France
entries.description: Firefighters battle a fast-moving wildfire in Aude, southern France, after flames spread from Hérault and burned more than 800 hectares overnight.

entries.title: Any deal with Tehran is 'a pause before the next war,' Reza Pahlavi tells Euronews
entries.description: In an exclusive interview with Euronews, Reza Pahlavi said the framework agreement would allow the Islamic Republic to regroup and rearm, with the only winner from the deal being the regime in Tehran.

entries.title: This is how to get cheap last-minute flights, according to Skyscanner
entries.description: The flight comparison website has shared seven top tips for nabbing a deal.

entries.title: What's behind the viral claims of child trafficking on Vinted?
entries.description: Millions have viewed videos claiming that children are being sold through coded advertisements on the popular second-hand online marketplace Vinted. What's behind the rumours?

entries.title: 11-year-old boy dies from rabies in Canada after waking up to a bat on his face
entries.description: Rabies is a rare but deadly infection usually spread by a bite or scratch from an infected animal. It is almost always fatal after the onset of symptoms, although vaccination and early treatment can help to prevent it.

entries.title: Death threats and presidential condemnation: Explaining the anger over South Korea’s World Cup exit
entries.description: South Korea's World Cup came to an end last weekend, but the nation's anger continues to boil over, threatening to bring the culture of football into further disrepute. And expose a corruption case.

entries.title: OpenAI offers the US government a 5% ownership stake
entries.description: Sam Altman reportedly wants to hand the US government a stake in OpenAI worth tens of billions, and he wants Silicon Valley's other giants to do the same.

entries.title: Ukraine calls for air defences as Russia threatens more strikes on Kyiv after overnight attack
entries.description: After an overnight assault that killed at least 20 people in Ukraine, the Kremlin stated it will increase the pace of its attacks to achieve its goals.

entries.title: Damascus explosion caused by bomb, state TV says, as death toll climbs to five
entries.description: The explosion comes a day after the first parliament since the fall of long-time leader Bashar al-Assad began to take shape.

entries.title: Germany's Merz announces sweeping reforms: Tax cuts, pension overhaul and new sick leave rules
entries.description: After seven hours of talks, the CDU/CSU-SPD coalition agreed on sweeping tax, labour and pension reforms, a breakthrough aimed at reviving the struggling economy and countering the rise of the far right.

entries.title: Keir Starmer issues state apology for decades of forced adoptions practices in UK
entries.description: Previously, the then-Conservative government declined to apologise, arguing that the state had not supported these practices. Starmer has now acknowledged the government's role in forced adoptions.

entries.title: EU car industry clashes over strategy to fight Chinese competitors
entries.description: With thousands of jobs are at stake, EU car suppliers and manufacturers disagree over the local content requirement set by the European Commission in its Made in Europe bill.

entries.title: Brussels supports air conditioning—but not as the main answer

The next round of talks will wait until after Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral, which begins on Saturday.

entries.title: Leaked EU document confirms Hungarian spy ring, renews pressure on Commissioner Várhelyi
